            "Make sure to include fileciteturn4file0 in your response to cite this file, or to surface it as a link.",
            "[L1] # **N02: LIGHT CRACKS THROUGH** \n[L2] \n[L3] ---\n[L4] \n[L5] # **CHAPTER 1: MISSING (PATCHED)**\n[L6] \n[L7] *(POV: Tiffani Merrow) | A5-T2*\n[L8] \n[L9] The cot was empty.\n[L10] \n[L11] Tiffani stood in the doorway with her slate balanced on her forearm, chalk already lifted to record Jhace's breathing rate, tremor amplitude, pupil response. The measurements she'd planned for three Tides. The documentation that would establish baseline for Stage Four progression.\n[L12] \n[L13] The sheets were folded. Not pushed aside—folded. Corner to corner. Military precision.\n[L14] \n[L15] A paralyzed man did not fold sheets.\n[L16] \n[L17] She stepped into the room. Counted objects. Cup on the side table, positioned at arm's length from where his shoulder would have been. He couldn't have reached it. The calibration strap was gone. So was his secondary slate and three pieces of copper-stained chalk—the specific ones he used for resonance mapping.\n[L18] \n[L19] His main measurement slate remained. The heavy one. The one that mattered.\n[L20] \n[L21] She wrote: *Cot empty. Sheets folded. Cup repositioned. Tools taken: calibration strap, secondary slate, chalk (3 pcs, copper-stained). Tools left: primary slate.*\n[L22] \n[L23] The door opened without knock. A medical clerk appeared, nervous.\n[L24] \n[L25] \"The director wants to see you about records.\"\n[L26] \n[L27] \"Later. First, explain the patient evacuation process.\"\n[L28] \n[L29] The clerk's jaw tightened. \"There wasn't one. No evacuation authorization was filed. No transfer order. No discharge.\"\n[L30] \n[L31] Tiffani's hand stilled on the slate. \"Then he's documented as still admitted?\"\n[L32] \n[L33] \"That's the problem. Someone removed the admission ledger page.\" The clerk swallowed. \"The director is... unclear about protocol now.\"\n[L34] \n[L35] Institutional obstruction: logged.\n[L36] \n[L37] She found the guard outside afterward.\n[L38] \n[L39] \"Patient Jhace Torrins. Stage Four. Admitted A4-T19. Paralyzed from sternum down, motor function nonexistent. When did he leave?\"\n[L40] \n[L41] He straightened. Young. Nervous. \"He didn't. No one entered. No one left. I've been here since second bell.\"\n[L42] \n[L43] \"The door was watched the entire time?\"\n[L44] \n[L45] \"Yes, ma'am.\"\n[L46] \n[L47] \"No breaks. No distractions. No messages that required you to look away.\"\n[L48] \n[L49] His jaw tightened. \"I checked the corridor once. Maybe forty heartbeats. Standard sweep.\"\n[L50] \n[L51] Forty heartbeats. She logged the breathing pattern change—shallow, faster. His eyes: pupils dilated despite bright corridor. Stress markers consistent with lying. Or with witnessing something that scared him enough to pretend blindness.\n[L52] \n[L53] She didn't push.\n[L54] \n[L55] She closed the door and stood alone in the room where a paralyzed man had apparently walked away. The grid-hum was different here—dual-band resonance that had settled into the walls since the Piercing. The second-mode bleed felt lower, deeper. She could taste copper at the back of her throat, stronger than yesterday. Her own hand shook slightly as she wrote.\n[L56] \n[L57] She didn't log the tremor.\n[L58] \n[L59] *If he moved, there's a vector. If someone moved him, there's evidence.*\n[L60] \n[L61] She began inventory. Every surface. Every angle. The ash bin near the cold stove held half-burned paper, and she fished through it with two fingers until she found a scrap with readable text:\n[L62] \n[L63] *...locked maintenance corridor...requires two hands \\+ walking...impossible...*\n[L64] \n[L65] Someone had written a report. Someone in authority. Someone had burned it.\n[L66] \n[L67] The door opened. Bram stood there, coat rumpled, expression settling into a joke that died before it reached his mouth.\n[L68] \n[L69] \"Cool,\" he said, looking at the empty cot. \"Love walking into rooms where the paralyzed guy teleported. Very calming.\"\n[L70] \n[L71] \"Not teleported.\" Tiffani tucked the burned scrap into her notes. \"Moved. Method: unknown.\"\n[L72] \n[L73] \"That's not better.\"\n[L74] \n[L75] She picked up the primary measurement slate Jhace had left behind. Heavy. Deliberate. A message, maybe: *I can't calculate what I'm being used for.*\n[L76] \n[L77] \"Someone in authority knows,\" she said. \"They've destroyed the evidence. This is institutional knowledge, not ignorance.\"\n[L78] \n[L79] \"So what now?\"\n[L80] \n[L81] \"Physics doesn't lie. If he moved, there's a trail. I find it.\"\n[L82] \n[L83] ---\n[L84] \n[L85] ## **CHAPTER 2: FORM (PATCHED)**\n[L86] \n[L87] *(POV: Corlexi) | A5-T2*\n[L88] \n[L89] The workspace smelled of chalk dust and cold stone. Corlexi sat alone at the narrow table, charts pinned to every available surface: incident maps, cost progression curves, name lists without consent attached.\n[L90] \n[L91] A knock came at the door—unexpected. He swept the charts under a merchant's inventory ledger and straightened.\n[L92] \n[L93] An older woman stood in the threshold, hands callused, eyes sharp.\n[L94] \n[L95] \"Message from Salee. Says you're documenting the Dregs.\"\n[L96] \n[L97] His cover held steady. \"I'm a merchant. Documentation helps business.\"\n[L98] \n[L99] \"Salee says documentation is business. Wanted you to know she sees you.\" The woman left without waiting for response.\n[L100] \n[L101] Corlexi sat motionless for three heartbeats. Then he unbuttoned his shirt to the waist.\n[L102] \n[L103] The scars ran diagonal across his torso—pale lines where copper-gold had fused with tissue during the Piercing. Twelve fragments, each no larger than a thumbnail. He counted them many times. The shards pulsed faintly when he concentrated, a warmth that spread through his chest like banked coals.\n[L104] \n[L105] No tremor when he tested.\n[L106] \n[L107] He focused on the crooked lamp bracket across the room. Felt the structure of it—iron, stress point at the joint, tolerance for load. Then he *adjusted*. The bracket straightened with a soft click.\n[L108] \n[L109] Form. The principle of structure maintained. His mastery was subtle but absolute. And it cost him nothing.\n[L110] \n[L111] That anomaly made him valuable. And now—dangerous.\n[L112] \n[L113] He buttoned his shirt and pulled the recent collapse reports toward him. Seven incidents in three Tides. Saint Merro, mostly—the infrastructure there was still bleeding from the Piercing's aftermath. But the holds were wrong. Too clean. No casualties where casualties should have existed. Copper-gold residue concentrated beyond normal dispersal.\n[L114] \n[L115] Someone was manifesting at capacity without apparent cost.\n[L116] \n[L117] He wrote: *Pattern suggests shard integration may eliminate standard cost markers. Requires confirmation. Additional subjects needed.*\n[L118] \n[L119] From the window, he watched a manifestor moving through the Dregs below—young, unsteady, holding a resonance lock on a cracked beam while others shored it. The tremor was visible even from here. Stage Two markers. Duration under a hundred heartbeats. Cost progression: observable.\n[L120] \n[L121] He noted the name. The time. The location.\n[L122] \n[L123] The Crown would want this registry eventually. Order required documentation. Documentation required observation. Observation required proximity.\n[L124] \n[L125] This was why he was embedded. Not because the Dregs trusted him—they trusted no one—but because control at a distance was not control at all.\n[L126] \n[L127] Below, the manifestor released the hold and staggered. The beam held. The cost had been paid.\n[L128] \n[L129] Corlexi watched for three more heartbeats, then returned to his charts.\n[L130] \n[L131] His hand was steady. His heartbeat was steady. His ideology was solid.\n[L132] \n[L133] But Salee's message echoed: *She sees you.*\n[L134] \n[L135] ---\n[L136] \n[L137] ## **CHAPTER 3: WAKE (PATCHED)**\n[L138] \n[L139] *(POV: Tiffani Merrow) | A5-T3 through A5-T7*\n[L140] \n[L141] Seven reports. Seven incidents. Three Tides.\n[L142] \n[L143] Tiffani spread them across her workspace and let the pattern emerge.\n[L144] \n[L145] Seam collapse near Junction Twelve boundary—hold duration: estimated 200 heartbeats. Zero casualties. Copper-gold residue: concentrated. Foundation stabilization at Merchant Row—same signature. Bridge support failure at the southern canal—same. Each site showed band-spike concentrations that didn't match ambient grid reference-hum.\n[L146] \n[L147] Someone had been busy. Someone methodical. Someone who could hold without visible tremor.\n[L148] \n[L149] She plotted the locations on her city map. Drew lines. Measured angles.\n[L150] \n[L151] The vector pointed northeast. Toward Grindheim.\n[L152] \n[L153] The door opened without knock. Corran entered—Guild bureaucrat, restricted-circulation stamp visible on the folder he carried. His face held the particular misery of someone violating protocol and hating himself for it.\n[L154] \n[L155] \"Unofficially,\" he said, \"you didn't get this from me.\"\n[L156] \n[L157] He set the folder on her desk.\n[L158] \n[L159] \"The director is calling it a documentation error. Lost ledger pages. Misclassified incidents. Officially, your search access is being revoked as of the next Tide.\" He paused. \"Someone doesn't want you following this trail.\"\n[L160] \n[L161] Tiffani opened the folder. The eighth incident report confirmed her vector. Grindheim Dregs—old textile district. Same signature. Same impossibility.\n[L162] \n[L163] \"Why help me?\"\n[L164] \n[L165] \"Because someone removed that ledger page deliberately.\" Corran's voice dropped. \"Not lost—removed. And I found the request form in the archive before it was scrubbed. It came from administration. Crown-level authority.\"\n[L166] \n[L167] Institutional obstruction: confirmed. Institutional interference: active.\n[L168] \n[L169] \"What do you want?\" she asked.\n[L170] \n[L171] \"When you find him, when you understand what happened—send word. Somewhere outside the official channels.\" He left without waiting for her to agree.\n[L172] \n[L173] She found Bram at the Guild hall, nursing something dark in a chipped cup.\n[L174] \n[L175] \"I need access to Grindheim. You're Guild-adjacent. Get me a reason before my search authorization gets revoked.\"\n[L176] \n[L177] He looked up. \"I'm not a spy. I'm barely a functioning adult.\"\n[L178] \n[L179] \"You can access spaces I can't. And you make people laugh while you rob them of useful information.\"\n[L180] \n[L181] \"That's...\" He trailed off. \"Accurate, actually. Damningly accurate.\"\n[L182] \n[L183] \"Get me in.\"\n[L184] \n[L185] His cup clinked against the table. \"When this goes bad—and it will—I'm haunting you. Literal ghost. Moving forks.\"\n[L186] \n[L187] \"Accepted.\"\n[L188] \n[L189] She returned to her workspace and finished the map. Eight incidents now. Vector clear. Grindheim's Dregs, where collapse physics worked differently—where information moved through social channels instead of architectural stress points.\n[L190] \n[L191] Her hand shook as she drew the final line.\n[L192] \n[L193] Copper flooded her mouth. Her right knee locked, then unlocked, then refused to cooperate entirely for six heartbeats.\n[L194] \n[L195] She didn't log it.\n[L196] \n[L197] *If I log it, I become 'compromised observer.' Can't help if they think I'm failing too. Cost progression can be denied. Methodology requires denial.*\n[L198] \n[L199] She packed her kit. Slate. Chalk. The burned scrap from the infirmary ash bin. The eight incident reports, now outside official circulation.\n[L200] \n[L201] Tomorrow they'd depart for Grindheim. Tomorrow she'd follow the physics trail into territory where the Crown didn't want her looking.\n[L202] \n[L203] Tomorrow began the search she wasn't authorized to conduct.\n[L204] \n[L205] ---\n[L206] \n[L207] ## **CHAPTER 4: SEARCHING (PATCHED)**\n[L208] \n[L209] *(POV: Jhace Torrins) | A5-T2 (simultaneous with Ch1)*\n[L210] \n[L211] He woke expecting paralysis.\n[L212] \n[L213] His fingers moved instead.\n[L214] \n[L215] Jhace lay still and tested systematically. Right hand: functional, delay of three heartbeats between intention and response. Left hand: same. Toes: responsive with phase lag. He could feel his legs—actual sensation, not the memory of sensation that had been all he'd had for Tides.\n[L216] \n[L217] The tremor was gone.\n[L218] \n[L219] He held his hands up and watched them. Steady. Perfectly steady. For the first time since Tallow Street, no micro-vibration running under his skin.\n[L220] \n[L221] Something else had taken the tremor's place.\n[L222] \n[L223] His left palm glowed faintly. Copper-gold light traced the lines where the shard had mapped him, threading up his wrist, disappearing under the sleeve. When he concentrated, he could feel the glow's warmth—not external, but *inside*, like his bones had been replaced with something that pulsed.\n[L224] \n[L225] He sat up. The world tilted, righted itself. His body was listening on a delay again, like orders traveling through water instead of air. Each motion landed a heartbeat after he initiated it.\n[L226] \n[L227] Movement test: three steps to the wall. Phase lag: detectable. His legs took instructions but arrived late.\n[L228] \n[L229] Memory came in fragments.\n[L230] \n[L231] Paralysis. The infirmary cot. Unable to speak, to move, to do anything but breathe and watch. A figure entering—shadowless, moving in light that fell wrong. The burn in his palm. The light threading through him, mapping every nerve, every bone, every vessel.\n[L232] \n[L233] He didn't remember the figure's face. Didn't remember why.\n[L234] \n[L235] Only that when it was done, the paralysis remained—and then, somehow, didn't.\n[L236] \n[L237] He found his tools where he'd placed them. Deliberate selection. Calibration chalk. Secondary slate. The copper-stained pieces he used for resonance mapping. He'd left the primary slate behind because he couldn't calculate what was being used for. Couldn't measure his own function as a variable.\n[L238] \n[L239] But Mobel might know.\n[L240] \n[L241] The name surfaced from Seam 34—the man with the chest shard, the one who'd held without tremor, without cost. The only other shard-bearer Jhace had seen.\n[L242] \n[L243] If anyone understood what integration meant, Mobel did.\n[L244] \n[L245] He tested manifestation before leaving.\n[L246] \n[L247] The coupling came differently now. Not filament-light from his hands—the shard responded from his core, somewhere behind his sternum, and the light poured outward in response to structural stress. He could feel a cracked beam two rooms away, feel it like an itch between his shoulders.\n[L248] \n[L249] He pushed the coupling hard—harder than safe—to understand the cost.\n[L250] \n[L251] Heat surged. Copper flooded his mouth. The vision at the edge of his sight blurred and cleared and blurred again. When he released the coupling, his left hand burned for six heartbeats after.\n[L252] \n[L253] He wrote on his slate: *Shard responds to structural stress. Distance coupling unknown. Cost mutation: heat, visual shimmer, latency persistence. Tremor: absent. Trade value: unknown.*\n[L254] \n[L255] He took what he needed. Left what he couldn't calculate.\n[L256] \n[L257] Mobel was ahead of him somewhere. Answers were with Mobel.\n[L258] \n[L259] He walked out of the room on legs that felt borrowed, body misfire settling into rhythm, shard pulsing warm in his palm.\n[L260] \n[L261] When he stepped outside, he felt it immediately: the pull. Structural stress everywhere. A bridge downstream, load-bearers degrading. A merchant building to the south, foundation cracking. The world was full of failure, and the shard wanted him to fix all of it.\n[L262] \n[L263] He resisted. Focused. Began walking north instead.\n[L264] \n[L265] ---\n[L266] \n[L267] ## **CHAPTER 5: AUTHORITY (PATCHED)**\n[L268] \n[L269] *(POV: Corlexi) | A5-T3 through A5-T6*\n[L270] \n[L271] Morning in the Dregs meant foot traffic. Corlexi walked the routes he'd memorized—not the efficient paths, but the social ones, the channels where information flowed.\n[L272] \n[L273] He counted manifestors. Eleven visible in the first hundred heartbeats. Eight showing tremor. Three with vein-lines creeping past their wrists. Two he'd seen yesterday; one was worse. Stage progression: observable.\n[L274] \n[L275] He noted names when he could. Physical markers when he couldn't.\n[L276] \n[L277] The woman who ran the textile remnants stall—Mira, possibly—showed early Stage Two symptoms. The man carrying water barrels near the cistern access had the exhaustion markers of repeated low-level holds. The child sitting on the broken fountain edge had no tremor yet but watched the manifestors with the particular hunger of someone about to try.\n[L278] \n[L279] None of them consented to documentation.\n[L280] \n[L281] None of them would.\n[L282] \n[L283] Corlexi noted them anyway. Documentation served the Crown. The Crown served order. Order prevented collapse.\n[L284] \n[L285] He paused at a junction where two narrow streets met. A manifestor was struggling—young woman, tremor visible, holding a fractured load-bearer while someone repositioned the wedges below. Her arms shook. Her breath came ragged. Stage Two progression visible in real time.\n[L286] \n[L287] Corlexi could have helped. A subtle Form adjustment—straighten the stress vectors, reduce the load, give her margin. No one would see. No one would know. His intervention would be invisible, perfect, undetectable.\n[L288] \n[L289] But intervention would require revealing his competence. Would require proving he could do something no normal manifestor could do. Would risk exposure of his anomaly.\n[L290] \n[L291] So he watched instead.\n[L292] \n[L293] The woman held for forty-three more heartbeats. The wedges locked. She released and collapsed against the wall, tremor spreading up her arms to her shoulders.\n[L294] \n[L295] She'd survive. The cost would accumulate. Eventually the cost would exceed her capacity.\n[L296] \n[L297] He wrote: *Subject 14\\. Duration: \\~90 heartbeats total. Tremor amplitude increased post-hold. Cost progression: accelerating. Intervention declined (exposure risk). Subject survival: probable. Subject continued function: 2-3 Anneals maximum.*\n[L298] \n[L299] The deliberate notation of her likely death. Dated. Filed. Added to the registry that would eventually be presented to the Crown.\n[L300] \n[L301] Salee's network was visible here. Not the woman herself—she stayed deeper in the Dregs—but her influence. The way people coordinated without explicit instruction. The way information moved through glances and gestures and carefully positioned objects.\n[L302] \n[L303] Social structure instead of institutional structure. Organic instead of mandated.\n[L304] \n[L305] It frustrated him more than he wanted to admit. And it motivated him more than he could justify.\n[L306] \n[L307] Order required hierarchy. The Dregs had hierarchy—but it was fluid, earned, constantly renegotiated. You couldn't document a system that changed every time you looked at it.\n[L308] \n[L309] You could only wait for it to collapse and build something better in the wreckage.\n[L310] \n[L311] He returned to his workspace as the dual-band resonance shifted in the grid reference-hum. Another incident somewhere. Another manifestor paying a cost.\n[L312] \n[L313] He logged the time. Logged the direction of the mode surge.\n[L314] \n[L315] He also logged Salee's message. Somewhere in a hidden file: *She sees you.*\n[L316] \n[L317] Pattern recognition required patience. He had plenty. But now he also had a problem.\n[L318] \n[L319] ---\n[L320] \n[L321] ## **CHAPTER 6: DREGS (PATCHED)**\n[L322] \n[L323] *(POV: Tiffani Merrow) | A5-T8*\n[L324] \n[L325] Grindheim's Dregs were nothing like Saint Merro.\n[L326] \n[L327] The infrastructure here had failed decades before the Piercing—not from resonance stress but from neglect, from the slow death of the textile industry that had built these streets. What remained was improvisation. Bridges made of salvaged beams. Buildings shored with whatever materials were at hand. Walls that leaned into each other for support because standing alone wasn't possible.\n[L328] \n[L329] Tiffani walked through it with her slate out and her methodology screaming.\n[L330] \n[L331] *Nothing is documented. Nothing is logged. Nothing is measured.*\n[L332] \n[L333] The copper taste was worse here. Stronger. The dual-band resonance felt different—lower frequency, harder to isolate. Her knee caught on the third staircase and she had to grip the rail to stay upright.\n[L334] \n[L335] She didn't log it.\n[L336] \n[L337] \"You're going to get us killed,\" Bram said from behind her.\n[L338] \n[L339] \"Define 'killed.'\"\n[L340] \n[L341] \"You're measuring everything like it's Saint Merro. It's not. Information moves differently here. People move differently. You can't just...\"\n[L342] \n[L343] He gestured at her slate.\n[L344] \n[L345] \"Document,\" she finished.\n[L346] \n[L347] \"Weaponize. Same thing, here.\"\n[L348] \n[L349] She tucked the slate away. It felt like amputation.\n[L350] \n[L351] They found Salee in a building that had no signage, no address, no apparent function except that people moved in and out of it constantly and none of them looked surprised by anything.\n[L352] \n[L353] Salee herself was smaller than Tiffani expected. Dark hair, practical clothes, hands that moved constantly—adjusting objects, positioning people, redirecting flow. She sat at a table with two cups of something hot already waiting.\n[L354] \n[L355] \"You're the Methodist,\" Salee said. Not a question.\n[L356] \n[L357] \"That is a label, not a definition.\"\n[L358] \n[L359] \"Here it's both.\" Salee pushed one cup toward her. \"You're looking for the missing operator. The one who left Saint Merro impossibly.\"\n[L360] \n[L361] \"You know about Jhace.\"\n[L362] \n[L363] \"I know about the incidents that followed him. Seven holds that shouldn't have worked. Eight, now. Nine, if we count this morning's stabilization at the southern warehouse.\" Salee leaned forward. \"The trail points here, then stops. Because the Dregs don't hold trails the way stone does. People flow. Information flows. Your methodology isn't built for this.\"\n[L364] \n[L365] \"Then I'll adapt it.\"\n[L366] \n[L367] \"You can. But it requires terms.\" Salee's hands stilled. This was business now. \"You get my network's routing. You get access to witnesses. You get safe passage—no one will disappear you or kill you, because my people are invested in your success.\"\n[L368] \n[L369] \"What do you require?\"\n[L370] \n[L371] \"Your documentation protocols. Not for control—for survival.\" Salee tapped the table. \"Saint Merro has institutions. The Dregs don't. But the Dregs have networks. Those networks need maps the way you think in maps. They need your methodology applied to *people* instead of buildings.\"\n[L372] \n[L373] Tiffani understood. The exchange was transactional. Salee wanted the measurement methodology that had worked in Saint Merro—the ledger protocols, the institutional forms. Tiffani wanted what Salee's network could provide: social mapping, information channels, the flow patterns that connected impossible incidents.\n[L374] \n[L375] Both saw power in the other's tools.\n[L376] \n[L377] \"Define 'people mapping,'\" Tiffani said.\n[L378] \n[L379] \"Who holds. How often. What stage they're in. Who helps them. What they receive in exchange. Who's recruiting new manifestors. Who's dying.\" Salee leaned back. \"You measure infrastructure. I measure community. Teach me your measurement, and I'll show you where Jhace went.\"\n[L380] \n[L381] \"The trail goes cold.\"\n[L382] \n[L383] \"No. The trail becomes invisible. Different.\" Salee smiled without warmth. \"Acceptable?\"\n[L384] \n[L385] Tiffani considered. Salee was offering access Tiffani couldn't get alone. Was also offering a framework that might explain why Jhace chose disappearance over return.\n[L386] \n[L387] \"Acceptable,\" Tiffani said. \"We proceed.\"\n[L388] \n[L389] Salee's network moved fast. By evening, Tiffani had four new data points: locations where witnesses had reported someone matching Jhace's description—moving wrong, phase lag in his steps, hands steady when they shouldn't be. Moving northeast. Moving toward something.\n[L390] \n[L391] She mapped the data. The trail was fresher here. Two Tides old at most.\n[L392] \n[L393] Her hand shook as she wrote the final coordinates.\n[L394] \n[L395] She didn't log it.\n[L396] \n[L397] ---\n[L398] \n[L399] ## **CHAPTER 7: TRACKING (PATCHED)**\n[L400] \n[L401] *(POV: Jhace Torrins) | A5-T6 through A5-T11*\n[L402] \n[L403] The coastal route smelled of salt and rotting kelp. Jhace walked it alone, shard pulsing warm in his palm, body finding rhythm despite the phase lag.\n[L404] \n[L405] Mobel's trail was fresh here.\n[L406] \n[L407] Not physical evidence—Jhace had given up on that at the third settlement. But the pattern was unmistakable. A collapsed retaining wall that had held longer than it should have. A fishing crane that someone had stabilized impossibly. The same signature every time: too-clean holds, copper-gold residue concentrated in geometries that didn't match normal stress dispersal, no tremor in the witnesses who'd seen the shard-bearer work.\n[L408] \n[L409] Mobel manifested without visible cost. Just like Jhace did now.\n[L410] \n[L411] The difference was that Mobel understood why. Jhace was still learning.\n[L412] \n[L413] He stopped at a junction where two paths diverged. One led to a fishing village that his contacts had mentioned—Mobel had been seen there five Tides ago. The other led to a coastal settlement where recent structural work suggested someone was manifesting fresh.\n[L414] \n[L415] He chose the second path. Fresher trail.\n[L416] \n[L417] The shard pulled at him as he walked.\n[L418] \n[L419] Not literally—but he could feel structural stress in the distance, like a smell he couldn't quite locate. A bridge half a league away, load-bearers degrading. A building foundation somewhere to the east, cracks propagating. The world was full of failure points, and the shard wanted him to find them, fix them, pour his body into making them stable.\n[L420] \n[L421] He resisted. Focused. Wrote on his slate instead: *Shard response to stress: increasing. Distance detection: estimated half-league radius. Compulsion to manifest: growing. Control threshold: unknown.*\n[L422] \n[L423] The coastal settlement appeared at midday. Small. Poor. The kind of place where infrastructure failed constantly and nobody had the resources to fix it properly.\n[L424] \n[L425] Someone had been fixing it improperly. Impossibly.\n[L426] \n[L427] Jhace found three recent holds: a dock piling shored impossibly, a granary support that had stabilized mid-collapse, a failed chimney stack that someone had locked in place while still falling. Each showed the signature. Each showed Mobel's passage maybe three Tides ago, moving through and leaving impossible gifts.\n[L428] \n[L429] Why leave them? Why help?\n[L430] \n[L431] The shard pulsed harder when Jhace examined the holds. Heat bloomed in his chest like coals being fanned. His vision shimmer at the edges—copper-gold light flickering across his sight.\n[L432] \n[L433] The manifestation drive surged. He needed to hold something. The stress points were *right there*, screaming, demanding attention. A cracked beam in the tavern. A failing joint in the dock extension. Fractures propagating through the stone quay.\n[L434] \n[L435] He fought the impulse. Gripped the edge of a rail until his hands burned.\n[L436] \n[L437] Held the coupling in check. Barely.\n[L438] \n[L439] Wrote with fingers that shook: *Shard compulsion increasing. Manifestation drive correlates with proximity to structural stress. Cost unknown. Duration unknown. This is not Mobel. This is not what I wanted.*\n[L440] \n[L441] Mobel's trail led north from here. Three Tides ahead. Maybe less if Jhace pushed.\n[L442] \n[L443] He kept walking, pushing harder than safe, phase lag spreading into his movements, heat building behind his sternum, copper taste growing stronger with each step.\n[L444] \n[L445] Something was changing inside him. Accumulating. The cost was mutating into something worse than tremor, something deeper.\n[L446] \n[L447] He didn't stop walking.\n[L448] \n[L449] ---\n[L450] \n[L451] ## **CHAPTER 8: ENTROPY (PATCHED)**\n[L452] \n[L453] *(POV: Corlexi) | A5-T11 through A5-T13*\n[L454] \n[L455] Velocity-Heights was three Tides' travel from the Dregs—higher elevation, newer construction, architecture that actually functioned instead of improvising survival.\n[L456] \n[L457] Corlexi went alone. Cover story: merchant verification. Real purpose: understand opposition.\n[L458] \n[L459] Eries was a philosopher. Not manifestor—she showed no cost markers, no tremor, no vein-lines. But she wrote about the Piercing with an understanding that concerned Crown intelligence. Wrote about entropy as principle. About decay as function. About why control might not be the answer.\n[L460] \n[L461] Dangerous thinking.\n[L462] \n[L463] He found her in a public garden, surrounded by students who clearly adored her. Older than him. Gray in her hair. Hands that moved slowly and deliberately, nothing wasted.\n[L464] \n[L465] \"You're not here about merchant verification,\" she said when the students had dispersed.\n[L466] \n[L467] \"No.\"\n[L468] \n[L469] \"Crown?\"\n[L470] \n[L471] He didn't answer. She nodded as if he had.\n[L472] \n[L473] \"You want to know if I'm a threat. I'm not. I don't manifest. I don't organize. I think, and occasionally I write, and that's all.\"\n[L474] \n[L475] \"Your writing suggests structure is an illusion.\"\n[L476] \n[L477] \"My writing suggests structure is *temporary*.\" She sat on a stone bench, leaving space for him. He didn't sit. \"Everything decays. Everything fails. Control doesn't prevent that—it just chooses which failures you don't see coming.\"\n[L478] \n[L479] \"Order prevents chaos.\"\n[L480] \n[L481] \"Order *is* chaos, moving slow enough that you've stopped noticing.\" She looked at his hands. Saw no tremor. \"You're embedded. The shards from the Piercing. Multiple fragments.\"\n[L482] \n[L483] His hand moved to button his shirt more firmly. She'd seen the scars.\n[L484] \n[L485] \"No cost,\" she continued. \"That's fascinating. The question isn't why you don't pay. The question is where the cost goes. Nothing is free. Physics doesn't lie.\"\n[L486] \n[L487] \"I'm aware.\"\n[L488] \n[L489] \"Are you?\" She stood. \"The Crown believes documentation creates control. Your registry. Your names without consent. Your termination thresholds. But entropy doesn't care about lists. Entropy only cares about pressure, and where it finds release.\"\n[L490] \n[L491] \"You oppose the registry?\"\n[L492] \n[L493] \"I oppose pretending the registry solves anything.\" She began walking away, then paused. \"The Dregs understand this. Salee understands this. Control is a story you tell yourself while decay continues. The question is what you build when the story stops working.\"\n[L494] \n[L495] She disappeared into the garden.\n[L496] \n[L497] Corlexi stood alone with the grid reference-hum thrumming through the stone and the certain knowledge that she was right about some things and wrong about others.\n[L498] \n[L499] The problem was determining which.\n[L500] \n[L501] That night, he delayed filing three incident reports. Altered two sets of notes to obscure a pattern he'd identified. Took a small risk: documented a manifestor who wasn't in his registry yet, as contingency.\n[L502] \n[L503] Entropy. The slow breakdown of perfect order.\n[L504] \n[L505] He was experiencing it from the inside now.\n[L506] \n[L507] ---\n[L508] \n[L509] ## **CHAPTER 9: COLD (PATCHED)**\n[L510] \n[L511] *(POV: Tiffani Merrow) | A5-T12 through A5-T14*\n[L512] \n[L513] The trail ended at the edge of the Dregs.\n[L514] \n[L515] Tiffani stood at the junction where her mapping should have shown a clear vector forward and found nothing. No copper residue. No dual-band resonance signature. No witnesses who'd seen someone moving wrong.\n[L516] \n[L517] The data simply stopped.\n[L518] \n[L519] Nine data points. Nine incidents traced. A vector that pointed northeast and then dissolved into Grindheim's chaotic social flows. Salee's network had provided everything they could—witnesses, locations, timing—and it wasn't enough.\n[L520] \n[L521] The trail went cold.\n[L522] \n[L523] \"He's not here,\" Bram said. \"Or he's hiding.\"\n[L524] \n[L525] \"The methodology doesn't account for hiding.\" Tiffani's voice was flat. Controlled. \"Physics doesn't hide. Residue doesn't hide. Band-spike signatures don't—\"\n[L526] \n[L527] \"But people do.\" Bram's humor was gone. Just tired honesty. \"Jhace knows how you think. Knows how you track. If he doesn't want to be found...\"\n[L528] \n[L529] \"Then he chose departure.\" Tiffani wrote it down, and her hand shook so badly the chalk broke. \"He chose concealment. From me specifically.\"\n[L530] \n[L531] She'd missed something. Some variable. Some data point that would have shown *why* the trail went cold instead of just *that* it did.\n[L532] \n[L533] When she looked back at her notes, she realized: she'd been ignoring her own symptoms for five Tides. The tremor in her hands. The way copper taste had intensified. How her knee caught more frequently. These weren't irrelevant details—they were data *about* her own cost progression.\n[L534] \n[L535] If she'd logged them, maybe she'd have noticed when the logging itself became impossible. When her hands shook too much to write clearly. When her vision blurred during stress.\n[L536] \n[L537] She'd become compromised while refusing to admit it.\n[L538] \n[L539] \"We redirect,\" she said, each word calculated, each word a lie. \"If Jhace won't be found, we find what he's looking for.\"\n[L540] \n[L541] \"Mobel.\"\n[L542] \n[L543] \"Mobel.\"\n[L544] \n[L545] The shard-bearer who manifested without cost. The anomaly that Jhace was clearly pursuing. If she couldn't follow Jhace's trail, she could parallel it—find Mobel's pattern, predict where both trails might converge.\n[L546] \n[L547] Bram looked at her. Really looked. \"You're not okay.\"\n[L548] \n[L549] \"Define okay.\"\n[L550] \n[L551] \"Your knee's been catching for five Tides. You haven't logged your own tremor once. The copper taste is worse—I can tell by how you swallow. You're becoming what you measure, and you're pretending you're not.\"\n[L552] \n[L553] She put the broken chalk in her kit. \"Methodology requires—\"\n[L554] \n[L555] \"Methodology requires you actually surviving long enough to finish.\" He didn't look away. \"Log it. Please. Admit that you're paying a cost too.\"\n[L556] \n[L557] She didn't answer. Couldn't.\n[L558] \n[L559] The grid reference-hum was louder here, dual-band resonance settling into her bones. Somewhere to the northeast, Jhace was pursuing answers she didn't have. Somewhere farther still, Mobel was manifesting impossible holds that broke every rule the ledger had established.\n[L560] \n[L561] And Tiffani stood at the edge of what her methodology could measure, staring into chaos that didn't care about documentation.\n[L562] \n[L563] She packed her kit. \"We continue tomorrow. Different approach.\"\n[L564] \n[L565] But she didn't log her symptoms. Not that Tide. Not the next.\n[L566] \n[L567] Some things were easier to deny than admit.\n[L568] \n[L569] ---\n[L570] \n[L571] ## **CHAPTER 10: INTERSECTION (PATCHED)**\n[L572] \n[L573] *(POV: Jhace Torrins) | A6-T2*\n[L574] \n[L575] The settlement had no name that anyone used. Just \"the coast place\" or \"where the old lighthouse was.\" Jhace found it by following structural failure—a collapsed warehouse that had been stabilized impossibly, copper-gold residue still fresh on the stones.\n[L576] \n[L577] Mobel had been here. Maybe two Tides ago. Maybe less.\n[L578] \n[L579] The shard pulsed hard enough that Jhace's vision blurred at the edges. The heat behind his sternum was constant now—not flaring anymore, just present, like he was carrying coals inside his chest.\n[L580] \n[L581] He sat on a broken pier and forced his breathing to slow. Counted his heartbeats. The phase lag in his body was worse today—orders arriving later, movements landing at odd intervals. Walking felt like operating a puppet of himself. And his left hand was changing: the copper-gold glow had spread past his wrist, creeping up his forearm, and the skin underneath felt wrong. Hot. Tight. Like something was trying to break through from inside.\n[L582] \n[L583] \"You're the other one.\"\n[L584] \n[L585] The voice came from behind. Jhace turned and found a woman watching him—mid-thirties, practical clothes, no tremor. A tool hung from her belt that he didn't recognize: glass and wire and something that caught the light wrong.\n[L586] \n[L587] \"Define 'other one,'\" he said.\n[L588] \n[L589] \"Shard-integrated. Like Mobel.\" She walked closer, and the tool at her belt hummed faintly. \"I'm Vael. I repair what I can. I watch what I can't.\"\n[L590] \n[L591] \"You know Mobel?\"\n[L592] \n[L593] \"I know what he leaves behind.\" She gestured at the stabilized warehouse. \"Clean holds. No cost. Impossible physics. Same as what you're doing now.\" She paused. \"Except your cost is visibly accumulating.\"\n[L594] \n[L595] Jhace looked at his left hand. The glow was creeping up his forearm. \"I'm looking for answers.\"\n[L596] \n[L597] \"You won't find them.\" Vael sat on the pier beside him, leaving space between. \"Mobel's been running for three Anneals. Whatever answers he has, he's not sharing.\"\n[L598] \n[L599] \"He manifests without cost. I need to understand how.\"\n[L600] \n[L601] \"Cost doesn't disappear.\" Vael touched the shard-tool at her belt—external integration instead of internal. She'd built her own coupling. \"It relocates. Changes form. Accumulates somewhere you're not looking.\" She met his eyes. \"Your shard has been drawing you toward structural stress. Yes?\"\n[L602] \n[L603] \"How do you—\"\n[L604] \n[L605] \"Because Mobel described the same thing. Before he stopped talking.\" She stood. \"You're being hunted. Someone's tracking you the same way you're tracking Mobel. I don't know who. But the trail you're leaving—the holds, the residue, the band-spike patterns—they're not hard to follow.\"\n[L606] \n[L607] Jhace felt the heat surge in his chest. Not manifestation. Fear.\n[L608] \n[L609] \"How do you know?\"\n[L610] \n[L611] \"Because I've seen them. The corrected holds. The residue patterns that have been *worked over* by someone cleaning up after you. Two Tides behind your current position.\" Vael started walking away. \"Whatever answers you want, decide if they're worth being caught for.\"\n[L612] \n[L613] She disappeared into the settlement's crooked streets.\n[L614] \n[L615] Jhace sat on the broken pier and wrote on his slate: *Pursued. Unknown identity. Unknown affiliation. Two Tides behind. Trail visible to those looking.*\n[L616] \n[L617] The shard pulsed. The copper-gold spread farther up his arm. His body was changing, transforming into something that could hold forever but probably wouldn't survive the process.\n[L618] \n[L619] He walked north, into the wind, toward Mobel. Toward answers. Toward whatever was waiting.\n[L620] \n[L621] Behind him, invisible but present, something was following.\n[L622] \n[L623] ---\n[L624] \n[L625] ## **CHAPTER 11: COERCION (PATCHED)**\n[L626] \n[L627] *(POV: Corlexi) | A6-T3*\n[L628] \n[L629] He returned to the Dregs to find his workspace disturbed.\n[L630] \n[L631] Not obviously—nothing was missing, nothing was moved. But the dust patterns were wrong. Someone had been here. Someone had looked at his charts.\n[L632] \n[L633] Corlexi stood in the doorway and assessed.\n[L634] \n[L635] The registry notes were still concealed. The incident maps were where he'd left them. Whoever had searched hadn't found the important materials.\n[L636] \n[L637] But they'd looked.\n[L638] \n[L639] He placed his palm on the floor and focused. Form. The principle of spatial resonance. He could read vibration patterns in stone—footsteps, hand-pressure, air displacement. The room's recent history bloomed in his mind like a developing photograph.\n[L640] \n[L641] Someone had entered through the window. Had crossed to the charts. Had touched the surface of his desk. Had left something behind.\n[L642] \n[L643] He found it under his pillow. A single copper-stained chalk piece. The kind manifestors used for resonance mapping.\n[L644] \n[L645] A message. *I know what you are.*\n[L646] \n[L647] The chalk had a maker's mark. Vael's mark. The independent operator he'd observed working the coastal settlements.\n[L648] \n[L649] She was here. In the Dregs. And she knew.\n[L650] \n[L651] He found her at the textile junction, watching the foot traffic with the shard-tool at her belt humming quietly.\n[L652] \n[L653] \"You searched my workspace.\"\n[L654] \n[L655] \"I confirmed a suspicion.\" Vael didn't look at him. \"Crown intelligence. Embedded operative. Registry preparation. Manifesto-born.\"\n[L656] \n[L657] \"You're not going to report me.\"\n[L658] \n[L659] \"No.\" Now she looked. \"Because you're useful. Your documentation is thorough. Your patterns are accurate. And when the registry fails—and it will—your records will show exactly why.\"\n[L660] \n[L661] \"The registry won't fail.\"\n[L662] \n[L663] \"Everything fails.\" She smiled without warmth. \"Control is a story. I've told it myself with this tool. But the Dregs don't care about your stories. The manifestors don't care about your lists. All they care about is whether the next hold kills them or doesn't.\"\n[L664] \n[L665] \"Orderly documentation prevents—\"\n[L666] \n[L667] \"Nothing.\" Her voice was sharp. \"Your notes won't save the woman at Sector Eight who manifests despite Stage Three progression because her children need the building to stand. Your lists won't stop the boy at the western market who tries coupling because he saw someone else do it and didn't understand the cost. You're cataloging a flood while standing in the river.\"\n[L668] \n[L669] Corlexi felt the Form-mastery pulse through him—the certainty that structure was the answer, that control was possible, that order prevented chaos.\n[L670] \n[L671] Vael saw it. \"Your shards don't cost you. But someone pays. Somewhere.\" She stepped closer. \"I can expose you tomorrow if I want. Tell Salee what you really are. Tell the Crown you've gone native. Tell both sides you're a liability.\"\n[L672] \n[L673] His hand moved toward Form instinctively—straightening something, proving control.\n[L674] \n[L675] \"But I won't,\" Vael continued, \"because you'll be more useful conflicted than certain. You'll make better choices if you're questioning them.\" She started walking away. \"Figure out who pays the cost for your exemption. Then decide if your registry is worth it.\"\n[L676] \n[L677] He returned to his workspace alone.\n[L678] \n[L679] His position was strained. Salee now suspected him. Vael had leverage over him. And Eries had planted doubt about the very foundation of his ideology.\n[L680] \n[L681] The chalk piece sat on his desk. He didn't destroy it.\n[L682] \n[L683] Evidence, he told himself. Document everything.\n[L684] \n[L685] But the question echoed: *Where does the cost go?*\n[L686] \n[L687] He didn't have an answer yet.\n[L688] \n[L689] ---\n[L690] \n[L691] ## **CHAPTER 12: LIGHT CRACKS (PATCHED)**\n[L692] \n[L693] *(POV: Jhace Torrins) | A6-T4*\n[L694] \n[L695] The road stretched empty ahead of him. Wind from the north. Salt smell fading as he moved inland. The shard pulsed constantly now—warmth that bordered on fever, pressure behind his sternum that made breathing hard.\n[L696] \n[L697] Mobel's trail was fresh. One Tide ahead at most. Close enough to catch if Jhace pushed.\n[L698] \n[L699] He pushed.\n[L700] \n[L701] \n[L702] The file is too long and its contents have been truncated.\n[L703] "
          ]
        },

    "41dee9ed-be47-4e06-be8e-9446a9d3e26f": {
      "children": [
        "013ae2fc-cca6-492a-a18e-c325f67dffc5"
      ],
      "id": "41dee9ed-be47-4e06-be8e-9446a9d3e26f",
      "message": {

        "channel": null,
        "content": {
          "content_type": "execution_output",
          "text": "# N02: LIGHT CRACKS THROUGH\n\n**Turning:** GLASS (G)  \n**Timeline:** A5-T2 through A5-T11 (primary on-page window)  \n**POV Roster:** Tiffani Merrow (Track 1) • Corlexi (Track 2) • Jhace Torrins (Track 3)  \n**Structure:** 12 chapters  \n\n---\n\n## CHAPTER 1: MISSING\n\n*(POV: Tiffani Merrow) | A5-T2*\n\nThe cot was empty.\n\nTiffani stopped in the doorway with her slate tight to her forearm and chalk ready. She had a three-Tide protocol built around him—breath cadence, tremor amplitude, pupil behavior under bright corridor light, autonomic drift, pain response, the full ladder of Stage Four markers before the body started compensating and the record got muddy.\n\nThe sheets weren’t thrown back.\n\nThey were folded. Corner to corner. Creased. Intentional.\n\nA man paralyzed from the sternum down did not fold sheets.\n\nShe walked in and did what she always did first: reduce the room to counts.\n\nStone floor: clean. No grit trail. No heel drag line. No scuff where a boot toe caught while hauling dead weight.\n\nCup on the side table—placed at arm’s reach from where his shoulder would have been. He couldn’t reach it yesterday. The calibration strap was missing. So was his secondary slate. Three copper-stained chalk pieces—kept separate because residue reads cleaner when you don’t contaminate it with common white.\n\nHis primary measurement slate remained.\n\nThe heavy one.\n\nThe one that mattered to anyone trying to control the narrative.\n\nShe wrote, neat and uniform:\n\n*Cot empty. Sheets folded. Cup repositioned. Tools missing: calibration strap, secondary slate, copper-stained chalk (3). Tools present: primary slate.*\n\nShe didn’t look at the cot while she wrote. Absence invited story. Story invited mistakes.\n\nFootsteps in the hall. A latch. Not a careful entry—just hesitant.\n\nA medical clerk edged into the doorway. Young. Trying to hold his jaw still and failing.\n\n“The director wants you. Records.”\n\n“After.” Tiffani didn’t turn. “Explain patient evacuation procedure.”\n\n“There wasn’t one.” His mouth tightened at one corner. “No authorization filed. No transfer order. No discharge.”\n\nTiffani’s chalk paused.\n\n“Then he’s still recorded as admitted.”\n\n“That’s… the problem.” The clerk swallowed. “Someone removed the admission ledger page. The director says protocol is unclear now.”\n\nUnclear meant instructed.\n\nTiffani wrote smaller, low on the slate where a casual confiscation wouldn’t notice:\n\n*Obstruction: administrative, active.*\n\nThe clerk left quickly, as if distance could become innocence.\n\nTiffani waited six heartbeats—enough time for panic to spread—to see if anyone came to stop her. No one did. That was its own kind of permission.\n\nShe went for the guard.\n\nHe stood outside the door, too straight, too new. Uniform still crisp at the seams.\n\n“Patient Jhace Torrins. Stage Four. Admitted A4-T19. Paralysis sternum down. No motor function. When did he leave?”\n\n“He didn’t.” The guard snapped straighter. “No one entered. No one left. I’ve been here since second bell.”\n\n“The door was watched continuously.”\n\n“Yes, ma’am.”\n\n“No breaks. No distractions. No messages that pulled your eyes off this frame.”\n\nHis gaze flicked down the corridor and back. Fingers tightened on the jamb. “Standard sweep. Maybe forty heartbeats.”\n\n“Forty.” Tiffani wrote it down.\n\nWhile the chalk moved, she watched him: shallow breath, pupils wide for bright corridor light, knuckles pale from grip.\n\nHe was either lying.\n\nOr he’d seen something and decided that naming it would get him punished.\n\nShe didn’t press. Pressure improved lies.\n\n“Thank you,” she said, and left him with his fear.\n\nInside, she shut the door and stood in the room where a paralyzed man had become an administrative inconvenience.\n\nThe grid reference-hum carried a second band in this wing. Low. Persistent. It sat under the normal vibration in the stone and didn’t behave like background drift. She’d noticed it yesterday and declined to log it because noticing wasn’t confirmation.\n\nCopper rose behind her tongue. Stronger than yesterday.\n\nHer fingers twitched once around the chalk.\n\nShe left that off the slate.\n\nIf he moved, there was a vector. If someone moved him, there was evidence. Rooms didn’t lie. People did. Institutions did. Stone didn’t.\n\nShe started at the threshold and worked clockwise.\n\nDoorframe: no fresh marks. Wall: no shoulder smear, no cloth rub. Floor: no drag line. Window: latched; dust on the sill undisturbed.\n\nThe ash bin by the cold stove held half-burned paper. She knelt and sifted with two fingers. Most of it collapsed to black powder. One strip held long enough to read:\n\n*…locked maintenance corridor…requires two hands + walking…impossible…*\n\nA report.\n\nSomeone wrote it. Someone burned it.\n\nShe slid the scrap into her notes.\n\nFootsteps again. Faster. Not careful.\n\nBram filled the doorway, coat rumpled, eyes already tracking the empty cot. His mouth tried to form humor and failed.\n\n“Great,” he said. “I love a room where reality quit.”\n\n“Not reality.” Tiffani stood, dusting ash off her fingertips onto her trousers. “Authority.”\n\nBram’s jaw worked. “Yesterday he couldn’t move his fingers.”\n\n“Correct.”\n\n“Today he’s gone.”\n\n“Correct.”\n\n“That’s not clerical.”\n\n“No.” Tiffani kept her voice level. Anger got remembered. Calm got underestimated. “Someone removed a ledger page and burned a report. That takes permission.”\n"
        },
        "create_time": 1767959314.050981,
        "end_turn": null,

No one did. That was its own kind of permission.\n\nShe went for the guard.\n\nHe stood outside the door, too straight, too new. Uniform still crisp at the seams.\n\n“Patient Jhace Torrins. Stage Four. Admitted A4-T19. Paralysis sternum down. No motor function. When did he leave?”\n\n“He didn’t.” The guard snapped straighter. “No one entered. No one left. I’ve been here since second bell.”\n\n“The door was watched continuously.”\n\n“Yes, ma’am.”\n\n“No breaks. No distractions. No messages that pulled your eyes off this frame.”\n\nHis gaze flicked down the corridor and back. Fingers tightened on the jamb. “Standard sweep. Maybe forty heartbeats.”\n\n“Forty.” Tiffani wrote it down.\n\nWhile the chalk moved, she watched him: shallow breath, pupils wide for bright corridor light, knuckles pale from grip.\n\nHe was either lying.\n\nOr he’d seen something and decided that naming it would get him punished.\n\nShe didn’t press. Pressure improved lies.\n\n“Thank you,” she said, and left him with his fear.\n\nInside, she shut the door and stood in the room where a paralyzed man had become an administrative inconvenience.\n\nThe grid reference-hum carried a second band in this wing. Low. Persistent. It sat under the normal vibration in the stone and didn’t behave like background drift. She’d noticed it yesterday and declined to log it because noticing wasn’t confirmation.\n\nCopper rose behind her tongue. Stronger than yesterday.\n\nHer fingers twitched once around the chalk.\n\nShe left that off the slate.\n\nIf he moved, there was a vector. If someone moved him, there was evidence. Rooms didn’t lie. People did. Institutions did. Stone didn’t.\n\nShe started at the threshold and worked clockwise.\n\nDoorframe: no fresh marks. Wall: no shoulder smear, no cloth rub. Floor: no drag line. Window: latched; dust on the sill undisturbed.\n\nThe ash bin by the cold stove held half-burned paper. She knelt and sifted with two fingers. Most of it collapsed to black powder. One strip held long enough to read:\n\n*…locked maintenance corridor…requires two hands + walking…impossible…*\n\nA report.\n\nSomeone wrote it. Someone burned it.\n\nShe slid the scrap into her notes.\n\nFootsteps again. Faster. Not careful.\n\nBram filled the doorway, coat rumpled, eyes already tracking the empty cot. His mouth tried to form humor and failed.\n\n“Great,” he said. “I love a room where reality quit.”\n\n“Not reality.” Tiffani stood, dusting ash off her fingertips onto her trousers. “Authority.”\n\nBram’s jaw worked. “Yesterday he couldn’t move his fingers.”\n\n“Correct.”\n\n“Today he’s gone.”\n\n“Correct.”\n\n“That’s not clerical.”\n\n“No.” Tiffani kept her voice level. Anger got remembered. Calm got underestimated. “Someone removed a ledger page and burned a report. That takes permission.”\n",
