            "## N02 Story + World Bible (Production-Locked)\n\n### Production identity\n- **Title:** *N02: Light Cracks Through* fileciteturn6file0L1-L2  \n- **Structure:** **12 chapters exactly** fileciteturn6file0L1-L2  \n- **Length target:** **12k–15k words** fileciteturn6file0L1-L2  \n- **Timeline coverage (applied):** **A5-T2 → ~A6-T4** (starts 1–2 Tides after N01 end) fileciteturn6file0L9-L11  \n- **POV roster (locked by you):** Tiffani (Track 1), Corlexi (Track 2), Jhace (Track 3)\n\n### Canon locks (non-negotiable)\n(These are your locks; this bible assumes them as hard constraints.)\n- Intruder identity stays undefined (no reveals, no hints)\n- No god-vessel awakenings (reserved for N5)\n- Tiffani does **not** find Jhace\n- Jhace does **not** find Mobel\n- Corlexi’s cover remains intact (strained is allowed)\n\n---\n\n## Global ruleset (applies to every chapter)\n\n### Timekeeping lock\n- **Format:** **Anneal X, Tide Y** (shorthand: **AX-TY**) fileciteturn6file0L4-L6  \n- **Definitions:** **Tide = day-count** from Piercing; **Anneal = 5-Tide grouping** fileciteturn6file0L7-L8  \n- **Anchor points:** N01 ends **A4-T21**; N02 starts **A5-T2** fileciteturn6file0L9-L11  \n\n### Lexicon lock (anti-music compliance)\n- “**doubled harmonic**” → **dual-band resonance** / **second-mode bleed** fileciteturn6file0L12-L14  \n- “**harmonic spikes**” → **band-spike** / **mode surge** fileciteturn6file0L13-L15  \n- “baseline” is allowed; prefer **reference hum** when it lands harder fileciteturn6file0L14-L16  \n\n### Stage marker anchor (cost progression ladder)\nUse this as the in-world “reference card” characters can think in without exposition-dumping.\n- **Stage One:** tremor post-hold, recovers within one Anneal fileciteturn6file0L20-L23  \n- **Stage Two:** tremor persistent; vein-lines creep; exhaustion accumulates fileciteturn6file0L22-L24  \n- **Stage Three:** vein-lines widespread; tremor worsens daily; paralysis-risk visible fileciteturn6file0L23-L25  \n- **Stage Four:** paralysis onset; autonomic instability; death within Crucible if untreated fileciteturn6file0L24-L25  \n\n### Cost law (the physics promise)\n- **No free power.** If it looks free, it’s either:\n  1) **misread cost** (paid elsewhere / later), or  \n  2) **anomaly** that creates a *bigger* narrative problem (Corlexi, Mobel, shard integration).  \n- Jhace’s “no tremor” is **not “no cost.”** It’s **cost mutation** (heat + latency + spread). fileciteturn6file0L260-L264  \n- Corlexi’s “no cost” is **mystery + risk**, not a solution. He can do subtle Form adjustments “invisible, perfect” but avoids it because exposure risk is real. fileciteturn6file0L295-L299  \n\n---\n\n## World state (N02-specific snapshot)\n\n### The grid after the Piercing\n- The ambient grid-hum now carries **dual-band resonance / second-mode bleed** in structures and bodies. Tiffani feels it in the infirmary walls and tastes copper with it. fileciteturn6file0L75-L77  \n- “Band-spike” events show up at incident sites—evidence of high-output coupling beyond normal stress dispersal. fileciteturn6file0L159-L162  \n\n### Two cities, two logics\n**Saint Merro (institution logic):**\n- Infirmary intake ledgers, authorization, record custody, “search access.”  \n- N02 opens with **institutional obstruction**: admission ledger page removed; director “unclear”; deliberate hiding. fileciteturn6file0L51-L55  \n- Corran confirms Crown-level interference and revocation pressure. fileciteturn6file0L173-L181  \n\n**Grindheim Dregs (network logic):**\n- Infrastructure is improvised; information is social, not archival. fileciteturn6file0L330-L337  \n- Salee’s power is routing: safe passage, witnesses, and community mapping (who holds / who dies). fileciteturn6file0L372-L385  \n- Corlexi’s Crown logic clashes with Dregs fluid hierarchy (“you can’t document a system that changes every time you look at it”). fileciteturn6file0L315-L317  \n\n### The coastal route (failure-point world)\n- The shard “pull” is basically structural stress-scenting: bridges, load-bearers, foundations calling to be fixed. fileciteturn6file0L421-L423  \n- Mobel’s wake is “impossible gifts”: clean holds, concentrated residue geometry, no visible tremor in witnesses. fileciteturn6file0L409-L413  \n\n---\n\n## Power systems in N02 (what’s different from N01)\n\n### Standard coupling (most manifestors)\n- Mechanic: resonance coupling to a structure → a “hold / lock” that temporarily overrides failure progression.\n- Observable cost: tremor, exhaustion, vein-lines; cost grows with duration and repetition (Stage ladder). fileciteturn6file0L281-L286  \n\n### Form (Corlexi)\n- Corlexi can “adjust” structure (lamp bracket test) with **no cost**—that anomaly is why he’s valuable and dangerous. fileciteturn6file0L124-L129  \n- He refuses visible intervention because competence = exposure. fileciteturn6file0L295-L299  \n- His Form also reads **spatial resonance history** (footsteps, pressure, displacement). fileciteturn6file0L629-L631  \n\n### Shard integration (Jhace, Mobel; Vael externalizes it)\n**Jhace (internal, forced/unknown procedure):**\n- Wakes mobile; tremor gone; **phase lag** appears (commands arriving late). fileciteturn6file0L226-L239  \n- Cost mutation: heat surge + visual shimmer + latency persistence after coupling. fileciteturn6file0L260-L264  \n- Compulsion: stress detection radius ~half-league; drive to “fix” increases. fileciteturn6file0L421-L424  \n- Physical transformation: copper-gold glow spreads up arm; by Ch12 it’s at elbow; rate roughly **handspan per Tide**. fileciteturn5file2L9-L12  \n\n**Mobel (internal, ahead, still unreachable):**\n- Leaves a consistent signature: impossible stabilizations + clean holds; “understands why,” but won’t share. fileciteturn6file0L411-L413  \n\n**Vael (external tool, engineered coupling):**\n- Carries a shard-tool (glass + wire + wrong light); “built her own coupling.” fileciteturn6file0L578-L599  \n- States the rule: **cost relocates**; doesn’t vanish. fileciteturn6file0L592-L599  \n\n---\n\n## Factions + pressure systems (who wants what)\n\n### Crown / Registry project (Corlexi’s mission)\n- Crown wants names, capability, termination thresholds; Corlexi documents non-consensually. fileciteturn6file0L140-L144  \n- N02 introduces internal corrosion: he delays filings and alters notes—Entropy begins in him. fileciteturn6file0L500-L504  \n\n### Guild / Saint Merro administration (Tiffani’s wall)\n- Authority hides Jhace’s removal: ledger page pulled, access revoked, “documentation error” cover. fileciteturn6file0L51-L55 fileciteturn6file0L173-L181  \n\n### Dregs network (Salee)\n- She trades routing + witnesses + safety for Tiffani’s documentation protocols applied to people (“who holds / who dies”). fileciteturn6file0L372-L385  \n\n### Philosophical opposition (Eries)\n- Eries attacks the fantasy of control: registry doesn’t stop decay; it just chooses blind spots. fileciteturn6file0L476-L493  \n- Key question she plants: **where does the cost go?** fileciteturn6file0L484-L489  \n\n### The pursuer / intruder (lock-compliant)\n- Jhace is being hunted by an unknown actor who “corrects holds” and cleans residue—two Tides behind. fileciteturn6file0L598-L606  \n- Intruder who “fixed” Jhace stays faceless/undefined; only “shadowless, light fell wrong” memory fragment is allowed. fileciteturn6file0L242-L245  \n\n---\n\n## Character bibles (N02 working dossiers)\n\n### Tiffani Merrow — “Method under pressure”\n**Role in N02:** investigator + systems engineer, trying to prove physics beats lies.  \n**Core want:** recover the missing variable (Jhace) and preserve methodology as truth.  \n**Core fear:** becoming compromised data—turning into the thing she measures.  \n**Voice rules:** clinical, inventory-based, vector language, refuses emotion until it leaks through omission.\n\n**Physical/cost thread:**  \n- Copper taste worsens; hand tremor starts; knee locks/catches; she refuses to log it. fileciteturn6file0L205-L212 fileciteturn6file0L529-L534  \n\n**Arc (12-ch shape):**\n1) Proves authority is lying (Jhace cannot have left; evidence burned). fileciteturn6file0L81-L86  \n2) Builds vector to Grindheim; loses institutional permissions; goes rogue. fileciteturn6file0L173-L181  \n3) Hits the Dregs where methodology fails unless adapted. fileciteturn6file0L368-L370  \n4) Accepts transactional alliance with Salee (method → people-mapping). fileciteturn6file0L372-L385  \n5) Trail goes cold; she must admit a new class of variable: will/hiding. fileciteturn6file0L521-L526  \n\n**Hard constraints:** She never meets Jhace; her best “win” is redefining the problem and setting up N03 pursuit logic.\n\n---\n\n### Jhace Torrins — “A walking cost mutation”\n**Role in N02:** mobile anomaly; chasing Mobel; becoming something else.  \n**Core want:** understand what was done to him (and why), by triangulating Mobel. fileciteturn6file0L250-L254  \n**Core fear:** losing agency to compulsion; being turned into a tool that “holds forever.” fileciteturn6file0L610-L612  \n**Voice rules:** terse, calibration language, writes like a tech logging a failing machine.\n\n**Mechanics thread:**\n- Phase lag: body obeys late. fileciteturn6file0L236-L239  \n- Coupling moved to core/sternum output, not hands. fileciteturn6file0L256-L259  \n- Heat behind sternum becomes constant by Ch10. fileciteturn6file0L572-L574  \n- Spread rate quantified by Ch12 (~handspan per Tide). fileciteturn5file2L9-L12  \n\n**Arc:**\n- Wakes “impossibly fixed,” chooses exile over returning to the system that’s lying. fileciteturn6file0L266-L270  \n- Tracks Mobel by signature, not sightings; compulsion to manifest rises as stress proximity rises. fileciteturn6file0L433-L441  \n- Learns he’s hunted; realizes his trail can be read and even “worked over.” fileciteturn6file0L598-L606  \n- Ends in a three-way chase with transformation accelerating. fileciteturn5file2L21-L24  \n\n**Hard constraints:** He never catches Mobel.\n\n---\n\n### Corlexi — “Order with hairline fractures”\n**Role in N02:** embedded Crown operative; registry architect; anomaly with “free” Form. fileciteturn6file0L124-L129  \n**Core want:** build control through documentation; prevent collapse via hierarchy. fileciteturn6file0L140-L144  \n**Core fear:** exposure; also (later) that Eries is right and order is just slow chaos. fileciteturn6file0L476-L493  \n\n**Tools/abilities:**\n- Subtle structure adjustment, costless (danger). fileciteturn6file0L124-L129  \n- Spatial resonance “read” of a room’s recent history. fileciteturn6file0L629-L631  \n\n**Arc:**\n- Sees Salee sees him (pressure on cover). fileciteturn6file0L112-L117  \n- Watches a manifestor suffer rather than risk exposure. fileciteturn6file0L295-L303  \n- Eries introduces entropy framework; he starts compromising his own registry discipline. fileciteturn6file0L500-L504  \n- Vael confronts him and holds leverage; cover strained but intact. fileciteturn5file4L31-L40  \n\n---\n\n### Salee — “Routing is power”\n- Runs Dregs navigation; not a mystic, not a vessel—pure coordination and exchange. fileciteturn6file0L356-L365  \n- Wants Tiffani’s protocols to map people, not buildings (“who holds / who dies”). fileciteturn6file0L382-L385  \n\n### Bram — “Comic relief with a spine”\n- His function is to say what Tiffani refuses to log; he names the denial. fileciteturn6file0L543-L552  \n- Keep him funny, but his best moments are when the humor stops.\n\n### Vael — “Independent operator; external shard-tech”\n- Built a shard-tool; understands trail-reading; warns about pursuit and cost relocation. fileciteturn6file0L592-L606  \n- She is a *pressure wedge* applied to Corlexi’s ideology and a mirror to Jhace’s situation.\n\n### Eries — “Entropy voice”\n- Non-manifestor philosopher; attacks “registry as solution.” fileciteturn6file0L472-L493  \n- Her function is not to win debates; it’s to infect certainty.\n\n### Corran — “Guilt inside the machine”\n- Confirms Crown-level interference; helps Tiffani off-channel. fileciteturn6file0L173-L185  \n\n### Mobel — “The unreachable answer”\n- Exists as wake/signature + motive engine. “Running for three Anneals.” fileciteturn5file0L19-L23  \n\n---\n\n## Locations bible (what each place *does* in the story)\n\n### Saint Merro infirmary (Ch1)\n**Function:** prove impossibility + prove institutional lying.  \n**Iconography:** folded sheets, missing strap/slate/chalk, burned report scrap, ledger page removed. fileciteturn6file0L37-L41 fileciteturn6file0L81-L86  \n**Sensory:** antiseptic + cold stove ash + copper taste + dual-band resonance in walls. fileciteturn6file0L75-L77  \n\n### Grindheim Dregs (Ch6, Ch9, Corlexi chapters)\n**Function:** force methodology evolution; show social routing vs institutional control. fileciteturn6file0L368-L377  \n**Iconography:** leaning buildings, salvaged bridges, no signage, people moving like a circulatory system. fileciteturn6file0L330-L337  \n\n### Coastal route + nameless settlements (Jhace track)\n**Function:** compulsion escalation; show Mobel wake; introduce Vael + the hunter. fileciteturn6file0L405-L413 fileciteturn6file0L598-L606  \n\n### Velocity-Heights (Ch8)\n**Function:** ideological attack on registry; “newer construction” = false comfort. fileciteturn6file0L454-L459  \n\n---\n\n## Plot architecture (12 chapter intent cards)\n\nUse these as the “why this chapter exists” reference while drafting/expanding.\n\n1) **Missing (Tiffani)** — establish absence + lie + physics trail premise. fileciteturn6file0L29-L36  \n2) **Form (Corlexi)** — show anomaly (no cost) + embed tension (“Salee sees you”). fileciteturn6file0L112-L117  \n3) **Wake (Tiffani)** — incident vector to Grindheim + Crown interference + go rogue. fileciteturn6file0L173-L181  \n4) **Searching (Jhace)** — wake mobile; log phase lag + heat; choose Mobel pursuit; intruder memory stays undefined. fileciteturn6file0L242-L245  \n5) **Authority (Corlexi)** — registry ethics on the ground; he watches suffering to protect cover. fileciteturn6file0L295-L303  \n6) **Dregs (Tiffani)** — Salee deal; methodology adapts from buildings → people. fileciteturn6file0L372-L385  \n7) **Tracking (Jhace)** — Mobel wake + compulsion escalation + stress-sense; keep him moving. fileciteturn6file0L433-L441  \n8) **Entropy (Corlexi)** — Eries infects certainty; Corlexi begins minor betrayals of registry purity. fileciteturn6file0L500-L504  \n9) **Cold (Tiffani)** — trail ends; introduce “will/hiding” variable; Bram confronts denial. fileciteturn6file0L521-L526  \n10) **Intersection (Jhace)** — Vael warns: hunted; cost relocates; pursuit two Tides behind. fileciteturn6file0L598-L606  \n11) **Coercion (Corlexi)** — Vael leverage; “I know what you are”; cover strained, not broken. fileciteturn5file4L1-L9  \n12) **Light Cracks (Jhace)** — involuntary core-output hold; spread rate quantified; three-way chase locks N03 hook. fileciteturn5file2L21-L24  \n\n---\n\n## Mystery ledger (lock-safe: what you may imply vs may not)\n\n### Allowed implications (good tension)\n- Jhace’s removal was **intentional and resourced** (ledger page removed; burned report; Crown pressure). fileciteturn6file0L51-L55 fileciteturn6file0L173-L181  \n- Intruder procedure changed Jhace’s cost shape (tremor replaced by heat/lag/spread). fileciteturn6file0L226-L234  \n- Someone is actively managing the trail behind Jhace (corrected holds; cleaned residue). fileciteturn6file0L598-L606  \n\n### Forbidden (breaks your canon locks)\n- Naming, describing, or “hinting” the intruder identity in a way readers could solve.\n- Any divine possession/vessel awakening beats.\n- Any scene where Tiffani and Jhace converge.\n- Any scene where Jhace catches Mobel.\n\n---\n\n## Voice + style bible (so the prose stays on-model)\n\n### Tiffani chapters: “ledger as morality”\n- Sentence behavior: short, precise, inventory cadence; emotional content appears as *withheld data* (“She didn’t log it.”) fileciteturn6file0L205-L212  \n- Preferred verbs: logged, measured, plotted, mapped, isolated, verified, constrained.  \n- Physical tells (keep repeating, escalating): copper taste, chalk break, knee lock, tremor suppression. fileciteturn6file0L525-L533  \n\n### Jhace chapters: “operator log under mutation”\n- Think like calibration notes turned into survival narration.\n- Repeat motifs: phase lag, heat behind sternum, stress-sense pull, compulsion, spread. fileciteturn6file0L572-L574 fileciteturn5file2L9-L12  \n\n### Corlexi chapters: “control language with hairline cracks”\n- Clean, managerial, quietly invasive.\n- His conflict is between “order prevents collapse” and the evidence that order is not preventing anything. fileciteturn6file0L476-L493  \n\n---\n\n## Continuity checklist (N02-specific)\n- Use AX-TY timestamps on chapter headers or internal references. fileciteturn6file0L4-L11  \n- Keep “dual-band resonance / second-mode bleed / band-spike / mode surge” consistent. fileciteturn6file0L12-L15  \n- Any “costless” event must be framed as anomaly/problem (Corlexi/Mobel), not a new baseline. fileciteturn6file0L124-L129  \n- Tiffani’s denial is a plot engine; Bram must call it out at least once (already does). fileciteturn6file0L543-L552  \n- End state must be **three-way chase** with Jhace transformation accelerating. fileciteturn5file2L21-L24  \n\n---\n\nIf you want, I can also produce a **scene-by-scene expansion blueprint** (2–5 beats per chapter, with required objects/sensory motifs/cost ticks) designed specifically to take the current ~6.5k draft up into the 12–15k target without adding new canon risk."
          ]
        },

        "end_turn": true,

            {
              "citation_format_type": "berry_file_search",
              "end_ix": 2117,
              "metadata": {
                "extra": {
                  "authors_display": null,
                  "cited_message_id": "7d2d851f-d7c8-4ba7-b0af-b8039bc5a676",
                  "cited_message_idx": 70,
                  "cloud_doc_url": null,
                  "doi": null,
                  "journal_homepage_url": null,
                  "journal_name": null,
                  "line_range": [
                    24,
                    25
                  ],
                  "publication_year": "None",
                  "retrieval_file_index": 0,
                  "retrieval_turn": 6,
                  "search_result_idx": null
                },
                "id": "file_000000007f2871f8b97dd923e8e90b92",
                "name": "N02 PRODUCTION HEADER.txt",
                "source": "my_files",
                "text": "Stage Three: Vein-lines widespread, tremor worsens daily, paralysis-risk visible\nStage Four: Paralysis onset, autonomic instability, death within Crucible if untreated",
                "type": "file"
              },
              "start_ix": 2088
            },
            {
              "citation_format_type": "berry_file_search",
              "end_ix": 2484,
              "metadata": {
                "extra": {
                  "authors_display": null,
                  "cited_message_id": "7d2d851f-d7c8-4ba7-b0af-b8039bc5a676",
                  "cited_message_idx": 70,
                  "cloud_doc_url": null,
                  "doi": null,
                  "journal_homepage_url": null,
                  "journal_name": null,
                  "line_range": [
                    260,
                    264
                  ],
                  "publication_year": "None",
                  "retrieval_file_index": 0,
                  "retrieval_turn": 6,
                  "search_result_idx": null
                },
                "id": "file_000000007f2871f8b97dd923e8e90b92",
                "name": "N02 PRODUCTION HEADER.txt",
                "source": "my_files",
                "text": "He pushed the coupling hard—harder than safe—to understand the cost.\nHeat surged. Copper flooded his mouth. The vision at the edge of his sight blurred and cleared and blurred again. When he released the coupling, his left hand burned for six heartbeats after.",
                "type": "file"
              },
              "start_ix": 2453
            },
            {
              "citation_format_type": "berry_file_search",
              "end_ix": 2679,
              "metadata": {
                "extra": {
                  "authors_display": null,
                  "cited_message_id": "7d2d851f-d7c8-4ba7-b0af-b8039bc5a676",
                  "cited_message_idx": 70,
                  "cloud_doc_url": null,
                  "doi": null,
                  "journal_homepage_url": null,
                  "journal_name": null,
                  "line_range": [
                    295,
                    299
                  ],
                  "publication_year": "None",
                  "retrieval_file_index": 0,
                  "retrieval_turn": 6,
                  "search_result_idx": null
                },
                "id": "file_000000007f2871f8b97dd923e8e90b92",
                "name": "N02 PRODUCTION HEADER.txt",
                "source": "my_files",
                "text": "Corlexi could have helped. A subtle Form adjustment—straighten the stress vectors, reduce the load, give her margin. No one would see. No one would know. His intervention would be invisible, perfect, undetectable.\nBut intervention would require revealing his competence. Would require proving he could do something no normal manifestor could do. Would risk exposure of his anomaly.",
                "type": "file"
              },
              "start_ix": 2648
            },
            {
              "citation_format_type": "berry_file_search",
              "end_ix": 2961,
              "metadata": {
                "extra": {
                  "authors_display": null,
                  "cited_message_id": "7d2d851f-d7c8-4ba7-b0af-b8039bc5a676",
                  "cited_message_idx": 70,
                  "cloud_doc_url": null,
                  "doi": null,
                  "journal_homepage_url": null,
                  "journal_name": null,
                  "line_range": [
                    75,
                    77
                  ],
                  "publication_year": "None",
                  "retrieval_file_index": 0,
                  "retrieval_turn": 6,
                  "search_result_idx": null
                },
                "id": "file_000000007f2871f8b97dd923e8e90b92",
                "name": "N02 PRODUCTION HEADER.txt",
                "source": "my_files",
                "text": "She closed the door and stood alone in the room where a paralyzed man had apparently walked away. The grid-hum was different here—dual-band resonance that had settled into the walls since the Piercing. The second-mode bleed felt lower, deeper. She could taste copper at the back of her throat, stronger than yesterday. Her own hand shook slightly as she wrote.\nShe didn't log the tremor.",
                "type": "file"
              },
              "start_ix": 2932
            },
            {
              "citation_format_type": "berry_file_search",
              "end_ix": 3108,
              "metadata": {
                "extra": {
                  "authors_display": null,
                  "cited_message_id": "7d2d851f-d7c8-4ba7-b0af-b8039bc5a676",
                  "cited_message_idx": 70,
                  "cloud_doc_url": null,
                  "doi": null,
                  "journal_homepage_url": null,
                  "journal_name": null,
                  "line_range": [
                    159,
                    162
                  ],
                  "publication_year": "None",
                  "retrieval_file_index": 0,
                  "retrieval_turn": 6,
                  "search_result_idx": null
                },
                "id": "file_000000007f2871f8b97dd923e8e90b92",
                "name": "N02 PRODUCTION HEADER.txt",
                "source": "my_files",
                "text": "Seam collapse near Junction Twelve boundary—hold duration: estimated 200 heartbeats. Zero casualties. Copper-gold residue: concentrated. Foundation stabilization at Merchant Row—same signature. Bridge support failure at the southern canal—same. Each site showed band-spike concentrations that didn't match ambient grid reference-hum.\nSomeone had been busy. Someone methodical. Someone who could hold without visible tremor.",
                "type": "file"
              },
              "start_ix": 3077
            },
            {
              "citation_format_type": "berry_file_search",
              "end_ix": 3401,
              "metadata": {
                "extra": {
                  "authors_display": null,
                  "cited_message_id": "7d2d851f-d7c8-4ba7-b0af-b8039bc5a676",
                  "cited_message_idx": 70,
                  "cloud_doc_url": null,
                  "doi": null,
                  "journal_homepage_url": null,
                  "journal_name": null,
                  "line_range": [
                    51,
                    55
                  ],
                  "publication_year": "None",
                  "retrieval_file_index": 0,
                  "retrieval_turn": 6,
                  "search_result_idx": null
                },
                "id": "file_000000007f2871f8b97dd923e8e90b92",
                "name": "N02 PRODUCTION HEADER.txt",
                "source": "my_files",
                "text": "Tiffani's hand stilled on the slate. \"Then he's documented as still admitted?\"\n\"That's the problem. Someone removed the admission ledger page.\" The clerk swallowed. \"The director is... unclear about protocol now.\"",
                "type": "file"
              },
              "start_ix": 3372
            },
            {
              "citation_format_type": "berry_file_search",
              "end_ix": 3503,
              "metadata": {
                "extra": {
                  "authors_display": null,
                  "cited_message_id": "7d2d851f-d7c8-4ba7-b0af-b8039bc5a676",
                  "cited_message_idx": 70,
                  "cloud_doc_url": null,
                  "doi": null,
                  "journal_homepage_url": null,
                  "journal_name": null,
                  "line_range": [
                    173,
                    181
                  ],
                  "publication_year": "None",
                  "retrieval_file_index": 0,
                  "retrieval_turn": 6,
                  "search_result_idx": null
                },
                "id": "file_000000007f2871f8b97dd923e8e90b92",
                "name": "N02 PRODUCTION HEADER.txt",
                "source": "my_files",
                "text": "\"The director is calling it a documentation error. Lost ledger pages. Misclassified incidents. Officially, your search access is being revoked as of the next Tide.\" He paused. \"Someone doesn't want you following this trail.\"\nTiffani opened the folder. The eighth incident report confirmed her vector. Grindheim Dregs—old textile district. Same signature. Same impossibility.",
                "type": "file"
              },
              "start_ix": 3472
            },
            {
              "citation_format_type": "berry_file_search",
              "end_ix": 3644,
              "metadata": {
                "extra": {
                  "authors_display": null,
                  "cited_message_id": "7d2d851f-d7c8-4ba7-b0af-b8039bc5a676",
                  "cited_message_idx": 70,
                  "cloud_doc_url": null,
                  "doi": null,
                  "journal_homepage_url": null,
                  "journal_name": null,
                  "line_range": [
                    330,
                    337
                  ],
                  "publication_year": "None",
                  "retrieval_file_index": 0,
                  "retrieval_turn": 6,
                  "search_result_idx": null
                },
                "id": "file_000000007f2871f8b97dd923e8e90b92",
                "name": "N02 PRODUCTION HEADER.txt",
                "source": "my_files",
                "text": "Grindheim's Dregs were nothing like Saint Merro.\nThe infrastructure here had failed decades before the Piercing—not from resonance stress but from neglect, from the slow death of the textile industry that had built these streets. What remained was improvisation. Bridges made of salvaged beams. Buildings shored with whatever materials were at hand. Walls that leaned into each other for support because standing alone wasn't possible.",
                "type": "file"
              },
              "start_ix": 3613
            },
            {
              "citation_format_type": "berry_file_search",
              "end_ix": 3777,
              "metadata": {
                "extra": {
                  "authors_display": null,
                  "cited_message_id": "7d2d851f-d7c8-4ba7-b0af-b8039bc5a676",
                  "cited_message_idx": 70,
                  "cloud_doc_url": null,
                  "doi": null,
                  "journal_homepage_url": null,
                  "journal_name": null,
                  "line_range": [
                    372,
                    385
                  ],
                  "publication_year": "None",
                  "retrieval_file_index": 0,
                  "retrieval_turn": 6,
                  "search_result_idx": null
                },
                "id": "file_000000007f2871f8b97dd923e8e90b92",
                "name": "N02 PRODUCTION HEADER.txt",
                "source": "my_files",
                "text": "\"You can. But it requires terms.\" Salee's hands stilled. This was business now. \"You get my network's routing. You get access to witnesses. You get safe passage—no one will disappear you or kill you, because my people are invested in your success.\"\n\"What do you require?\"",
                "type": "file"
              },
              "start_ix": 3746
            },
            {
              "citation_format_type": "berry_file_search",
              "end_ix": 3942,
              "metadata": {
                "extra": {
                  "authors_display": null,
                  "cited_message_id": "7d2d851f-d7c8-4ba7-b0af-b8039bc5a676",
                  "cited_message_idx": 70,
                  "cloud_doc_url": null,
                  "doi": null,
                  "journal_homepage_url": null,
                  "journal_name": null,
                  "line_range": [
                    315,
                    317
                  ],
                  "publication_year": "None",
                  "retrieval_file_index": 0,
                  "retrieval_turn": 6,
                  "search_result_idx": null
                },
                "id": "file_000000007f2871f8b97dd923e8e90b92",
                "name": "N02 PRODUCTION HEADER.txt",
                "source": "my_files",
                "text": "Order required hierarchy. The Dregs had hierarchy—but it was fluid, earned, constantly renegotiated. You couldn't document a system that changed every time you looked at it.\nYou could only wait for it to collapse and build something better in the wreckage.",
                "type": "file"
              },
              "start_ix": 3911
            },
            {
              "citation_format_type": "berry_file_search",
              "end_ix": 4137,
              "metadata": {
                "extra": {
                  "authors_display": null,
                  "cited_message_id": "7d2d851f-d7c8-4ba7-b0af-b8039bc5a676",
                  "cited_message_idx": 70,
                  "cloud_doc_url": null,
                  "doi": null,
                  "journal_homepage_url": null,
                  "journal_name": null,
                  "line_range": [
                    421,
                    423
                  ],
                  "publication_year": "None",
                  "retrieval_file_index": 0,
                  "retrieval_turn": 6,
                  "search_result_idx": null
                },
                "id": "file_000000007f2871f8b97dd923e8e90b92",
                "name": "N02 PRODUCTION HEADER.txt",
                "source": "my_files",
                "text": "Not literally—but he could feel structural stress in the distance, like a smell he couldn't quite locate. A bridge half a league away, load-bearers degrading. A building foundation somewhere to the east, cracks propagating. The world was full of failure points, and the shard wanted him to find them, fix them, pour his body into making them stable.\nHe resisted. Focused. Wrote on his slate instead: Shard response to stress: increasing. Distance detection: estimated half-league radius. Compulsion to manifest: growing. Control threshold: unknown.",
                "type": "file"
              },
              "start_ix": 4106
            },
            {
              "citation_format_type": "berry_file_search",
              "end_ix": 4285,
              "metadata": {
                "extra": {
                  "authors_display": null,
                  "cited_message_id": "7d2d851f-d7c8-4ba7-b0af-b8039bc5a676",
                  "cited_message_idx": 70,
                  "cloud_doc_url": null,
                  "doi": null,
                  "journal_homepage_url": null,
                  "journal_name": null,
                  "line_range": [
                    409,
                    413
                  ],
                  "publication_year": "None",
                  "retrieval_file_index": 0,
                  "retrieval_turn": 6,
                  "search_result_idx": null
                },
                "id": "file_000000007f2871f8b97dd923e8e90b92",
                "name": "N02 PRODUCTION HEADER.txt",
                "source": "my_files",
                "text": "Not physical evidence—Jhace had given up on that at the third settlement. But the pattern was unmistakable. A collapsed retaining wall that had held longer than it should have. A fishing crane that someone had stabilized impossibly. The same signature every time: too-clean holds, copper-gold residue concentrated in geometries that didn't match normal stress dispersal, no tremor in the witnesses who'd seen the shard-bearer work.\nMobel manifested without visible cost. Just like Jhace did now.",
                "type": "file"
              },
              "start_ix": 4254
            },
            {
              "citation_format_type": "berry_file_search",
              "end_ix": 4638,
              "metadata": {
                "extra": {
                  "authors_display": null,
                  "cited_message_id": "7d2d851f-d7c8-4ba7-b0af-b8039bc5a676",
                  "cited_message_idx": 70,
                  "cloud_doc_url": null,
                  "doi": null,
                  "journal_homepage_url": null,
                  "journal_name": null,
                  "line_range": [
                    281,
                    286
                  ],
                  "publication_year": "None",
                  "retrieval_file_index": 0,
                  "retrieval_turn": 6,
                  "search_result_idx": null
                },
                "id": "file_000000007f2871f8b97dd923e8e90b92",
                "name": "N02 PRODUCTION HEADER.txt",
                "source": "my_files",
                "text": "He counted manifestors. Eleven visible in the first hundred heartbeats. Eight showing tremor. Three with vein-lines creeping past their wrists. Two he'd seen yesterday; one was worse. Stage progression: observable.\nHe noted names when he could. Physical markers when he couldn't.",
                "type": "file"
              },
              "start_ix": 4607
            },
            {
              "citation_format_type": "berry_file_search",
              "end_ix": 4811,
              "metadata": {
                "extra": {
                  "authors_display": null,
                  "cited_message_id": "7d2d851f-d7c8-4ba7-b0af-b8039bc5a676",
                  "cited_message_idx": 70,
                  "cloud_doc_url": null,
                  "doi": null,
                  "journal_homepage_url": null,
                  "journal_name": null,
                  "line_range": [
                    124,
                    129
                  ],
                  "publication_year": "None",
                  "retrieval_file_index": 0,
                  "retrieval_turn": 6,
                  "search_result_idx": null
                },
                "id": "file_000000007f2871f8b97dd923e8e90b92",
                "name": "N02 PRODUCTION HEADER.txt",
                "source": "my_files",
                "text": "He focused on the crooked lamp bracket across the room. Felt the structure of it—iron, stress point at the joint, tolerance for load. Then he adjusted. The bracket straightened with a soft click.\nForm. The principle of structure maintained. His mastery was subtle but absolute. And it cost him nothing.",
                "type": "file"
              },
              "start_ix": 4780
            },
            {
              "citation_format_type": "berry_file_search",
              "end_ix": 4910,
              "metadata": {
                "extra": {
                  "authors_display": null,
                  "cited_message_id": "7d2d851f-d7c8-4ba7-b0af-b8039bc5a676",
                  "cited_message_idx": 70,
                  "cloud_doc_url": null,
                  "doi": null,
                  "journal_homepage_url": null,
                  "journal_name": null,
                  "line_range": [
                    295,
                    299
                  ],
                  "publication_year": "None",
                  "retrieval_file_index": 0,
                  "retrieval_turn": 6,
                  "search_result_idx": null
                },
                "id": "file_000000007f2871f8b97dd923e8e90b92",
                "name": "N02 PRODUCTION HEADER.txt",
                "source": "my_files",
                "text": "Corlexi could have helped. A subtle Form adjustment—straighten the stress vectors, reduce the load, give her margin. No one would see. No one would know. His intervention would be invisible, perfect, undetectable.\nBut intervention would require revealing his competence. Would require proving he could do something no normal manifestor could do. Would risk exposure of his anomaly.",
                "type": "file"
              },
              "start_ix": 4879
            },
            {
              "citation_format_type": "berry_file_search",
              "end_ix": 5033,
              "metadata": {
                "extra": {
                  "authors_display": null,
                  "cited_message_id": "7d2d851f-d7c8-4ba7-b0af-b8039bc5a676",
                  "cited_message_idx": 70,
                  "cloud_doc_url": null,
                  "doi": null,
                  "journal_homepage_url": null,
                  "journal_name": null,
                  "line_range": [
                    629,
                    631
                  ],
                  "publication_year": "None",
                  "retrieval_file_index": 0,
                  "retrieval_turn": 6,
                  "search_result_idx": null
                },
                "id": "file_000000007f2871f8b97dd923e8e90b92",
                "name": "N02 PRODUCTION HEADER.txt",
                "source": "my_files",
                "text": "He placed his palm on the floor and focused. Form. The principle of spatial resonance. He could read vibration patterns in stone—footsteps, hand-pressure, air displacement. The room's recent history bloomed in his mind like a developing photograph.\nSomeone had entered through the window. Had crossed to the charts. Had touched the surface of his desk. Had left something behind.",
                "type": "file"
              },
              "start_ix": 5002
            },
            {
              "citation_format_type": "berry_file_search",
              "end_ix": 5252,
              "metadata": {
                "extra": {
                  "authors_display": null,
                  "cited_message_id": "7d2d851f-d7c8-4ba7-b0af-b8039bc5a676",
                  "cited_message_idx": 70,
                  "cloud_doc_url": null,
                  "doi": null,
                  "journal_homepage_url": null,
                  "journal_name": null,
                  "line_range": [
                    226,
                    239
                  ],
                  "publication_year": "None",
                  "retrieval_file_index": 0,
                  "retrieval_turn": 6,
                  "search_result_idx": null
                },
                "id": "file_000000007f2871f8b97dd923e8e90b92",
                "name": "N02 PRODUCTION HEADER.txt",
                "source": "my_files",
                "text": "Jhace lay still and tested systematically. Right hand: functional, delay of three heartbeats between intention and response. Left hand: same. Toes: responsive with phase lag. He could feel his legs—actual sensation, not the memory of sensation that had been all he'd had for Tides.\nThe tremor was gone.",
                "type": "file"
              },
              "start_ix": 5221
            },
            {
              "citation_format_type": "berry_file_search",
              "end_ix": 5369,
              "metadata": {
                "extra": {
                  "authors_display": null,
                  "cited_message_id": "7d2d851f-d7c8-4ba7-b0af-b8039bc5a676",
                  "cited_message_idx": 70,
                  "cloud_doc_url": null,
                  "doi": null,
                  "journal_homepage_url": null,
                  "journal_name": null,
                  "line_range": [
                    260,
                    264
                  ],
                  "publication_year": "None",
                  "retrieval_file_index": 0,
                  "retrieval_turn": 6,
                  "search_result_idx": null
                },
                "id": "file_000000007f2871f8b97dd923e8e90b92",
                "name": "N02 PRODUCTION HEADER.txt",
                "source": "my_files",
                "text": "He pushed the coupling hard—harder than safe—to understand the cost.\nHeat surged. Copper flooded his mouth. The vision at the edge of his sight blurred and cleared and blurred again. When he released the coupling, his left hand burned for six heartbeats after.",
                "type": "file"
              },
              "start_ix": 5338
            },
            {
              "citation_format_type": "berry_file_search",
              "end_ix": 5481,
              "metadata": {
                "extra": {
                  "authors_display": null,
                  "cited_message_id": "7d2d851f-d7c8-4ba7-b0af-b8039bc5a676",
                  "cited_message_idx": 70,
                  "cloud_doc_url": null,
                  "doi": null,
                  "journal_homepage_url": null,
                  "journal_name": null,
                  "line_range": [
                    421,
                    424
                  ],
                  "publication_year": "None",
                  "retrieval_file_index": 0,
                  "retrieval_turn": 6,
                  "search_result_idx": null
                },
                "id": "file_000000007f2871f8b97dd923e8e90b92",
                "name": "N02 PRODUCTION HEADER.txt",
                "source": "my_files",
                "text": "Not literally—but he could feel structural stress in the distance, like a smell he couldn't quite locate. A bridge half a league away, load-bearers degrading. A building foundation somewhere to the east, cracks propagating. The world was full of failure points, and the shard wanted him to find them, fix them, pour his body into making them stable.\nHe resisted. Focused. Wrote on his slate instead: Shard response to stress: increasing. Distance detection: estimated half-league radius. Compulsion to manifest: growing. Control threshold: unknown.",
                "type": "file"
              },
              "start_ix": 5450
            },
            {
              "citation_format_type": "berry_file_search",
              "end_ix": 5631,
              "metadata": {
                "extra": {
                  "authors_display": null,
                  "cited_message_id": "b7f3abaa-13b5-450f-903a-0edae8516c93",
                  "cited_message_idx": 67,
                  "cloud_doc_url": null,
                  "doi": null,
                  "journal_homepage_url": null,
                  "journal_name": null,
                  "line_range": [
                    9,
                    12
                  ],
                  "publication_year": "None",
                  "retrieval_file_index": 2,
                  "retrieval_turn": 5,
                  "search_result_idx": 2
                },
                "id": "file_00000000be0471f8be6b30e111ea6ee7",
                "name": "N02_ LIGHT CRACKS THROUGH (1).md",
                "source": "my_files",
                "text": "He looked at his left hand. The copper-gold glow had spread to his elbow. The skin underneath was pale, almost translucent, like something was happening at a level his eyes couldn't quite focus on.\n*Cost mutation visible. Spread rate: \\~handspan per Tide. Termination threshold: unknown. This is not what I chose.*",
                "type": "file"
              },
              "start_ix": 5603
            },
            {
              "citation_format_type": "berry_file_search",
              "end_ix": 5824,
              "metadata": {
                "extra": {
                  "authors_display": null,
                  "cited_message_id": "7d2d851f-d7c8-4ba7-b0af-b8039bc5a676",
                  "cited_message_idx": 70,
                  "cloud_doc_url": null,
                  "doi": null,
                  "journal_homepage_url": null,
                  "journal_name": null,
                  "line_range": [
                    411,
                    413
                  ],
                  "publication_year": "None",
                  "retrieval_file_index": 0,
                  "retrieval_turn": 6,
                  "search_result_idx": null
                },
                "id": "file_000000007f2871f8b97dd923e8e90b92",
                "name": "N02 PRODUCTION HEADER.txt",
                "source": "my_files",
                "text": "Mobel manifested without visible cost. Just like Jhace did now.\nThe difference was that Mobel understood why. Jhace was still learning.",
                "type": "file"
              },
              "start_ix": 5793
            },
            {
              "citation_format_type": "berry_file_search",
              "end_ix": 5985,
              "metadata": {
                "extra": {
                  "authors_display": null,
                  "cited_message_id": "7d2d851f-d7c8-4ba7-b0af-b8039bc5a676",
                  "cited_message_idx": 70,
                  "cloud_doc_url": null,
                  "doi": null,
                  "journal_homepage_url": null,
                  "journal_name": null,
                  "line_range": [
                    578,
                    599
                  ],
                  "publication_year": "None",
                  "retrieval_file_index": 0,
                  "retrieval_turn": 6,
                  "search_result_idx": null
                },
                "id": "file_000000007f2871f8b97dd923e8e90b92",
                "name": "N02 PRODUCTION HEADER.txt",
                "source": "my_files",
                "text": "The voice came from behind. Jhace turned and found a woman watching him—mid-thirties, practical clothes, no tremor. A tool hung from her belt that he didn't recognize: glass and wire and something that caught the light wrong.\n\"Define 'other one,'\" he said.",
                "type": "file"
              },
              "start_ix": 5954
            },
            {
              "citation_format_type": "berry_file_search",
              "end_ix": 6074,
              "metadata": {
                "extra": {
                  "authors_display": null,
                  "cited_message_id": "7d2d851f-d7c8-4ba7-b0af-b8039bc5a676",
                  "cited_message_idx": 70,
                  "cloud_doc_url": null,
                  "doi": null,
                  "journal_homepage_url": null,
                  "journal_name": null,
                  "line_range": [
                    592,
                    599
                  ],
                  "publication_year": "None",
                  "retrieval_file_index": 0,
                  "retrieval_turn": 6,
                  "search_result_idx": null
                },
                "id": "file_000000007f2871f8b97dd923e8e90b92",
                "name": "N02 PRODUCTION HEADER.txt",
                "source": "my_files",
                "text": "\"He manifests without cost. I need to understand how.\"\n\"Cost doesn't disappear.\" Vael touched the shard-tool at her belt—external integration instead of internal. She'd built her own coupling. \"It relocates. Changes form. Accumulates somewhere you're not looking.\" She met his eyes. \"Your shard has been drawing you toward structural stress. Yes?\"",
                "type": "file"
              },
              "start_ix": 6043
            },
            {
              "citation_format_type": "berry_file_search",
              "end_ix": 6305,
              "metadata": {
                "extra": {
                  "authors_display": null,
                  "cited_message_id": "7d2d851f-d7c8-4ba7-b0af-b8039bc5a676",
                  "cited_message_idx": 70,
                  "cloud_doc_url": null,
                  "doi": null,
                  "journal_homepage_url": null,
                  "journal_name": null,
                  "line_range": [
                    140,
                    144
                  ],
                  "publication_year": "None",
                  "retrieval_file_index": 0,
                  "retrieval_turn": 6,
                  "search_result_idx": null
                },
                "id": "file_000000007f2871f8b97dd923e8e90b92",
                "name": "N02 PRODUCTION HEADER.txt",
                "source": "my_files",
                "text": "The Crown would want this registry eventually. Order required documentation. Documentation required observation. Observation required proximity.\nThis was why he was embedded. Not because the Dregs trusted him—they trusted no one—but because control at a distance was not control at all.",
                "type": "file"
              },
              "start_ix": 6274
            },
            {
              "citation_format_type": "berry_file_search",
              "end_ix": 6434,
              "metadata": {
                "extra": {
                  "authors_display": null,
                  "cited_message_id": "7d2d851f-d7c8-4ba7-b0af-b8039bc5a676",
                  "cited_message_idx": 70,
                  "cloud_doc_url": null,
                  "doi": null,
                  "journal_homepage_url": null,
                  "journal_name": null,
                  "line_range": [
                    500,
                    504
                  ],
                  "publication_year": "None",
                  "retrieval_file_index": 0,
                  "retrieval_turn": 6,
                  "search_result_idx": null
                },
                "id": "file_000000007f2871f8b97dd923e8e90b92",
                "name": "N02 PRODUCTION HEADER.txt",
                "source": "my_files",
                "text": "That night, he delayed filing three incident reports. Altered two sets of notes to obscure a pattern he'd identified. Took a small risk: documented a manifestor who wasn't in his registry yet, as contingency.\nEntropy. The slow breakdown of perfect order.",
                "type": "file"
              },
              "start_ix": 6403
            },
            {
              "citation_format_type": "berry_file_search",
              "end_ix": 6623,
              "metadata": {
                "extra": {
                  "authors_display": null,
                  "cited_message_id": "7d2d851f-d7c8-4ba7-b0af-b8039bc5a676",
                  "cited_message_idx": 70,
                  "cloud_doc_url": null,
                  "doi": null,
                  "journal_homepage_url": null,
                  "journal_name": null,
                  "line_range": [
                    51,
                    55
                  ],
                  "publication_year": "None",
                  "retrieval_file_index": 0,
                  "retrieval_turn": 6,
                  "search_result_idx": null
                },
                "id": "file_000000007f2871f8b97dd923e8e90b92",
                "name": "N02 PRODUCTION HEADER.txt",
                "source": "my_files",
                "text": "Tiffani's hand stilled on the slate. \"Then he's documented as still admitted?\"\n\"That's the problem. Someone removed the admission ledger page.\" The clerk swallowed. \"The director is... unclear about protocol now.\"",
                "type": "file"
              },
              "start_ix": 6594
            },
            {
              "citation_format_type": "berry_file_search",
              "end_ix": 6655,
              "metadata": {
                "extra": {
                  "authors_display": null,
                  "cited_message_id": "7d2d851f-d7c8-4ba7-b0af-b8039bc5a676",
                  "cited_message_idx": 70,
                  "cloud_doc_url": null,
                  "doi": null,
                  "journal_homepage_url": null,
                  "journal_name": null,
                  "line_range": [
                    173,
                    181
                  ],
                  "publication_year": "None",
                  "retrieval_file_index": 0,
                  "retrieval_turn": 6,
                  "search_result_idx": null
                },
                "id": "file_000000007f2871f8b97dd923e8e90b92",
                "name": "N02 PRODUCTION HEADER.txt",
                "source": "my_files",
                "text": "\"The director is calling it a documentation error. Lost ledger pages. Misclassified incidents. Officially, your search access is being revoked as of the next Tide.\" He paused. \"Someone doesn't want you following this trail.\"\nTiffani opened the folder. The eighth incident report confirmed her vector. Grindheim Dregs—old textile district. Same signature. Same impossibility.",
                "type": "file"
              },
              "start_ix": 6624
            },
            {
              "citation_format_type": "berry_file_search",
              "end_ix": 6840,
              "metadata": {
                "extra": {
                  "authors_display": null,
                  "cited_message_id": "7d2d851f-d7c8-4ba7-b0af-b8039bc5a676",
                  "cited_message_idx": 70,
                  "cloud_doc_url": null,
                  "doi": null,
                  "journal_homepage_url": null,
                  "journal_name": null,
                  "line_range": [
                    372,
                    385
                  ],
                  "publication_year": "None",
                  "retrieval_file_index": 0,
                  "retrieval_turn": 6,
                  "search_result_idx": null
                },
                "id": "file_000000007f2871f8b97dd923e8e90b92",
                "name": "N02 PRODUCTION HEADER.txt",
                "source": "my_files",
                "text": "\"You can. But it requires terms.\" Salee's hands stilled. This was business now. \"You get my network's routing. You get access to witnesses. You get safe passage—no one will disappear you or kill you, because my people are invested in your success.\"\n\"What do you require?\"",
                "type": "file"
              },
              "start_ix": 6809
            },
            {
              "citation_format_type": "berry_file_search",
              "end_ix": 7010,
              "metadata": {
                "extra": {
                  "authors_display": null,
                  "cited_message_id": "7d2d851f-d7c8-4ba7-b0af-b8039bc5a676",
                  "cited_message_idx": 70,
                  "cloud_doc_url": null,
                  "doi": null,
                  "journal_homepage_url": null,
                  "journal_name": null,
                  "line_range": [
                    476,
                    493
                  ],
                  "publication_year": "None",
                  "retrieval_file_index": 0,
                  "retrieval_turn": 6,
                  "search_result_idx": null
                },
                "id": "file_000000007f2871f8b97dd923e8e90b92",
                "name": "N02 PRODUCTION HEADER.txt",
                "source": "my_files",
                "text": "\"My writing suggests structure is temporary.\" She sat on a stone bench, leaving space for him. He didn't sit. \"Everything decays. Everything fails. Control doesn't prevent that—it just chooses which failures you don't see coming.\"\n\"Order prevents chaos.\"",
                "type": "file"
              },
              "start_ix": 6979
            },
            {
              "citation_format_type": "berry_file_search",
              "end_ix": 7099,
              "metadata": {
                "extra": {
                  "authors_display": null,
                  "cited_message_id": "7d2d851f-d7c8-4ba7-b0af-b8039bc5a676",
                  "cited_message_idx": 70,
                  "cloud_doc_url": null,
                  "doi": null,
                  "journal_homepage_url": null,
                  "journal_name": null,
                  "line_range": [
                    484,
                    489
                  ],
                  "publication_year": "None",
                  "retrieval_file_index": 0,
                  "retrieval_turn": 6,
                  "search_result_idx": null
                },
                "id": "file_000000007f2871f8b97dd923e8e90b92",
                "name": "N02 PRODUCTION HEADER.txt",
                "source": "my_files",
                "text": "\"No cost,\" she continued. \"That's fascinating. The question isn't why you don't pay. The question is where the cost goes. Nothing is free. Physics doesn't lie.\"\n\"I'm aware.\"",
                "type": "file"
              },
              "start_ix": 7068
            },
            {
              "citation_format_type": "berry_file_search",
              "end_ix": 7280,
              "metadata": {
                "extra": {
                  "authors_display": null,
                  "cited_message_id": "7d2d851f-d7c8-4ba7-b0af-b8039bc5a676",
                  "cited_message_idx": 70,
                  "cloud_doc_url": null,
                  "doi": null,
                  "journal_homepage_url": null,
                  "journal_name": null,
                  "line_range": [
                    598,
                    606
                  ],
                  "publication_year": "None",
                  "retrieval_file_index": 0,
                  "retrieval_turn": 6,
                  "search_result_idx": null
                },
                "id": "file_000000007f2871f8b97dd923e8e90b92",
                "name": "N02 PRODUCTION HEADER.txt",
                "source": "my_files",
                "text": "\"Because Mobel described the same thing. Before he stopped talking.\" She stood. \"You're being hunted. Someone's tracking you the same way you're tracking Mobel. I don't know who. But the trail you're leaving—the holds, the residue, the band-spike patterns—they're not hard to follow.\"\nJhace felt the heat surge in his chest. Not manifestation. Fear.",
                "type": "file"
              },
              "start_ix": 7249
            },
            {
              "citation_format_type": "berry_file_search",
              "end_ix": 7433,
              "metadata": {
                "extra": {
                  "authors_display": null,
                  "cited_message_id": "7d2d851f-d7c8-4ba7-b0af-b8039bc5a676",
                  "cited_message_idx": 70,
                  "cloud_doc_url": null,
                  "doi": null,
                  "journal_homepage_url": null,
                  "journal_name": null,
                  "line_range": [
                    242,
                    245
                  ],
                  "publication_year": "None",
                  "retrieval_file_index": 0,
                  "retrieval_turn": 6,
                  "search_result_idx": null
                },
                "id": "file_000000007f2871f8b97dd923e8e90b92",
                "name": "N02 PRODUCTION HEADER.txt",
                "source": "my_files",
                "text": "Paralysis. The infirmary cot. Unable to speak, to move, to do anything but breathe and watch. A figure entering—shadowless, moving in light that fell wrong. The burn in his palm. The light threading through him, mapping every nerve, every bone, every vessel.\nHe didn't remember the figure's face. Didn't remember why.",
                "type": "file"
              },
              "start_ix": 7402
            },
            {
              "citation_format_type": "berry_file_search",
              "end_ix": 8045,
              "metadata": {
                "extra": {
                  "authors_display": null,
                  "cited_message_id": "7d2d851f-d7c8-4ba7-b0af-b8039bc5a676",
                  "cited_message_idx": 70,
                  "cloud_doc_url": null,
                  "doi": null,
                  "journal_homepage_url": null,
                  "journal_name": null,
                  "line_range": [
                    205,
                    212
                  ],
                  "publication_year": "None",
                  "retrieval_file_index": 0,
                  "retrieval_turn": 6,
                  "search_result_idx": null
                },
                "id": "file_000000007f2871f8b97dd923e8e90b92",
                "name": "N02 PRODUCTION HEADER.txt",
                "source": "my_files",
                "text": "Her hand shook as she drew the final line.\nCopper flooded her mouth. Her right knee locked, then unlocked, then refused to cooperate entirely for six heartbeats.",
                "type": "file"
              },
              "start_ix": 8014
            },
            {
              "citation_format_type": "berry_file_search",
              "end_ix": 8077,
              "metadata": {
                "extra": {
                  "authors_display": null,
                  "cited_message_id": "7d2d851f-d7c8-4ba7-b0af-b8039bc5a676",
                  "cited_message_idx": 70,
                  "cloud_doc_url": null,
                  "doi": null,
                  "journal_homepage_url": null,
                  "journal_name": null,
                  "line_range": [
                    529,
                    534
                  ],
                  "publication_year": "None",
                  "retrieval_file_index": 0,
                  "retrieval_turn": 6,
                  "search_result_idx": null
                },
                "id": "file_000000007f2871f8b97dd923e8e90b92",
                "name": "N02 PRODUCTION HEADER.txt",
                "source": "my_files",
                "text": "When she looked back at her notes, she realized: she'd been ignoring her own symptoms for five Tides. The tremor in her hands. The way copper taste had intensified. How her knee caught more frequently. These weren't irrelevant details—they were data about her own cost progression.\nIf she'd logged them, maybe she'd have noticed when the logging itself became impossible. When her hands shook too much to write clearly. When her vision blurred during stress.",
                "type": "file"
              },
              "start_ix": 8046
            },
            {
              "citation_format_type": "berry_file_search",
              "end_ix": 8205,
              "metadata": {
                "extra": {
                  "authors_display": null,
                  "cited_message_id": "7d2d851f-d7c8-4ba7-b0af-b8039bc5a676",
                  "cited_message_idx": 70,
                  "cloud_doc_url": null,
                  "doi": null,
                  "journal_homepage_url": null,
                  "journal_name": null,
                  "line_range": [
                    81,
                    86
                  ],
                  "publication_year": "None",
                  "retrieval_file_index": 0,
                  "retrieval_turn": 6,
                  "search_result_idx": null
                },
                "id": "file_000000007f2871f8b97dd923e8e90b92",
                "name": "N02 PRODUCTION HEADER.txt",
                "source": "my_files",
                "text": "She began inventory. Every surface. Every angle. The ash bin near the cold stove held half-burned paper, and she fished through it with two fingers until she found a scrap with readable text:\n...locked maintenance corridor...requires two hands + walking...impossible...",
                "type": "file"
              },
              "start_ix": 8176
            },
            {
              "citation_format_type": "berry_file_search",
              "end_ix": 8315,
              "metadata": {
                "extra": {
                  "authors_display": null,
                  "cited_message_id": "7d2d851f-d7c8-4ba7-b0af-b8039bc5a676",
                  "cited_message_idx": 70,
                  "cloud_doc_url": null,
                  "doi": null,
                  "journal_homepage_url": null,
                  "journal_name": null,
                  "line_range": [
                    173,
                    181
                  ],
                  "publication_year": "None",
                  "retrieval_file_index": 0,
                  "retrieval_turn": 6,
                  "search_result_idx": null
                },
                "id": "file_000000007f2871f8b97dd923e8e90b92",
                "name": "N02 PRODUCTION HEADER.txt",
                "source": "my_files",
                "text": "\"The director is calling it a documentation error. Lost ledger pages. Misclassified incidents. Officially, your search access is being revoked as of the next Tide.\" He paused. \"Someone doesn't want you following this trail.\"\nTiffani opened the folder. The eighth incident report confirmed her vector. Grindheim Dregs—old textile district. Same signature. Same impossibility.",
                "type": "file"
              },
              "start_ix": 8284
            },
            {
              "citation_format_type": "berry_file_search",
              "end_ix": 8407,
              "metadata": {
                "extra": {
                  "authors_display": null,
                  "cited_message_id": "7d2d851f-d7c8-4ba7-b0af-b8039bc5a676",
                  "cited_message_idx": 70,
                  "cloud_doc_url": null,
                  "doi": null,
                  "journal_homepage_url": null,
                  "journal_name": null,
                  "line_range": [
                    368,
                    370
                  ],
                  "publication_year": "None",
                  "retrieval_file_index": 0,
                  "retrieval_turn": 6,
                  "search_result_idx": null
                },
                "id": "file_000000007f2871f8b97dd923e8e90b92",
                "name": "N02 PRODUCTION HEADER.txt",
                "source": "my_files",
                "text": "\"I know about the incidents that followed him. Seven holds that shouldn't have worked. Eight, now. Nine, if we count this morning's stabilization at the southern warehouse.\" Salee leaned forward. \"The trail points here, then stops. Because the Dregs don't hold trails the way stone does. People flow. Information flows. Your methodology isn't built for this.\"\n\"Then I'll adapt it.\"",
                "type": "file"
              },
              "start_ix": 8376
            },
            {
              "citation_format_type": "berry_file_search",
              "end_ix": 8513,
              "metadata": {
                "extra": {
                  "authors_display": null,
                  "cited_message_id": "7d2d851f-d7c8-4ba7-b0af-b8039bc5a676",
                  "cited_message_idx": 70,
                  "cloud_doc_url": null,
                  "doi": null,
                  "journal_homepage_url": null,
                  "journal_name": null,
                  "line_range": [
                    372,
                    385
                  ],
                  "publication_year": "None",
                  "retrieval_file_index": 0,
                  "retrieval_turn": 6,
                  "search_result_idx": null
                },
                "id": "file_000000007f2871f8b97dd923e8e90b92",
                "name": "N02 PRODUCTION HEADER.txt",
                "source": "my_files",
                "text": "\"You can. But it requires terms.\" Salee's hands stilled. This was business now. \"You get my network's routing. You get access to witnesses. You get safe passage—no one will disappear you or kill you, because my people are invested in your success.\"\n\"What do you require?\"",
                "type": "file"
              },
              "start_ix": 8482
            },
            {
              "citation_format_type": "berry_file_search",
              "end_ix": 8620,
              "metadata": {
                "extra": {
                  "authors_display": null,
                  "cited_message_id": "7d2d851f-d7c8-4ba7-b0af-b8039bc5a676",
                  "cited_message_idx": 70,
                  "cloud_doc_url": null,
                  "doi": null,
                  "journal_homepage_url": null,
                  "journal_name": null,
                  "line_range": [
                    521,
                    526
                  ],
                  "publication_year": "None",
                  "retrieval_file_index": 0,
                  "retrieval_turn": 6,
                  "search_result_idx": null
                },
                "id": "file_000000007f2871f8b97dd923e8e90b92",
                "name": "N02 PRODUCTION HEADER.txt",
                "source": "my_files",
                "text": "\"The methodology doesn't account for hiding.\" Tiffani's voice was flat. Controlled. \"Physics doesn't hide. Residue doesn't hide. Band-spike signatures don't—\"\n\"But people do.\" Bram's humor was gone. Just tired honesty. \"Jhace knows how you think. Knows how you track. If he doesn't want to be found...\"",
                "type": "file"
              },
              "start_ix": 8589
            },
            {
              "citation_format_type": "berry_file_search",
              "end_ix": 8984,
              "metadata": {
                "extra": {
                  "authors_display": null,
                  "cited_message_id": "7d2d851f-d7c8-4ba7-b0af-b8039bc5a676",
                  "cited_message_idx": 70,
                  "cloud_doc_url": null,
                  "doi": null,
                  "journal_homepage_url": null,
                  "journal_name": null,
                  "line_range": [
                    250,
                    254
                  ],
                  "publication_year": "None",
                  "retrieval_file_index": 0,
                  "retrieval_turn": 6,
                  "search_result_idx": null
                },
                "id": "file_000000007f2871f8b97dd923e8e90b92",
                "name": "N02 PRODUCTION HEADER.txt",
                "source": "my_files",
                "text": "But Mobel might know.\nThe name surfaced from Seam 34—the man with the chest shard, the one who'd held without tremor, without cost. The only other shard-bearer Jhace had seen.",
                "type": "file"
              },
              "start_ix": 8953
            },
            {
              "citation_format_type": "berry_file_search",
              "end_ix": 9109,
              "metadata": {
                "extra": {
                  "authors_display": null,
                  "cited_message_id": "7d2d851f-d7c8-4ba7-b0af-b8039bc5a676",
                  "cited_message_idx": 70,
                  "cloud_doc_url": null,
                  "doi": null,
                  "journal_homepage_url": null,
                  "journal_name": null,
                  "line_range": [
                    610,
                    612
                  ],
                  "publication_year": "None",
                  "retrieval_file_index": 0,
                  "retrieval_turn": 6,
                  "search_result_idx": null
                },
                "id": "file_000000007f2871f8b97dd923e8e90b92",
                "name": "N02 PRODUCTION HEADER.txt",
                "source": "my_files",
                "text": "The shard pulsed. The copper-gold spread farther up his arm. His body was changing, transforming into something that could hold forever but probably wouldn't survive the process.\nHe walked north, into the wind, toward Mobel. Toward answers. Toward whatever was waiting.",
                "type": "file"
              },
              "start_ix": 9078
            },
            {
              "citation_format_type": "berry_file_search",
              "end_ix": 9288,
              "metadata": {
                "extra": {
                  "authors_display": null,
                  "cited_message_id": "7d2d851f-d7c8-4ba7-b0af-b8039bc5a676",
                  "cited_message_idx": 70,
                  "cloud_doc_url": null,
                  "doi": null,
                  "journal_homepage_url": null,
                  "journal_name": null,
                  "line_range": [
                    236,
                    239
                  ],
                  "publication_year": "None",
                  "retrieval_file_index": 0,
                  "retrieval_turn": 6,
                  "search_result_idx": null
                },
                "id": "file_000000007f2871f8b97dd923e8e90b92",
                "name": "N02 PRODUCTION HEADER.txt",
                "source": "my_files",
                "text": "He sat up. The world tilted, righted itself. His body was listening on a delay again, like orders traveling through water instead of air. Each motion landed a heartbeat after he initiated it.\nMovement test: three steps to the wall. Phase lag: detectable. His legs took instructions but arrived late.",
                "type": "file"
              },
              "start_ix": 9257
            },
            {
              "citation_format_type": "berry_file_search",
              "end_ix": 9374,
              "metadata": {
                "extra": {
                  "authors_display": null,
                  "cited_message_id": "7d2d851f-d7c8-4ba7-b0af-b8039bc5a676",
                  "cited_message_idx": 70,
                  "cloud_doc_url": null,
                  "doi": null,
                  "journal_homepage_url": null,
                  "journal_name": null,
                  "line_range": [
                    256,
                    259
                  ],
                  "publication_year": "None",
                  "retrieval_file_index": 0,
                  "retrieval_turn": 6,
                  "search_result_idx": null
                },
                "id": "file_000000007f2871f8b97dd923e8e90b92",
                "name": "N02 PRODUCTION HEADER.txt",
                "source": "my_files",
                "text": "He tested manifestation before leaving.\nThe coupling came differently now. Not filament-light from his hands—the shard responded from his core, somewhere behind his sternum, and the light poured outward in response to structural stress. He could feel a cracked beam two rooms away, feel it like an itch between his shoulders.",
                "type": "file"
              },
              "start_ix": 9343
            },
            {
              "citation_format_type": "berry_file_search",
              "end_ix": 9456,
              "metadata": {
                "extra": {
                  "authors_display": null,
                  "cited_message_id": "7d2d851f-d7c8-4ba7-b0af-b8039bc5a676",
                  "cited_message_idx": 70,
                  "cloud_doc_url": null,
                  "doi": null,
                  "journal_homepage_url": null,
                  "journal_name": null,
                  "line_range": [
                    572,
                    574
                  ],
                  "publication_year": "None",
                  "retrieval_file_index": 0,
                  "retrieval_turn": 6,
                  "search_result_idx": null
                },
                "id": "file_000000007f2871f8b97dd923e8e90b92",
                "name": "N02 PRODUCTION HEADER.txt",
                "source": "my_files",
                "text": "The shard pulsed hard enough that Jhace's vision blurred at the edges. The heat behind his sternum was constant now—not flaring anymore, just present, like he was carrying coals inside his chest.\nHe sat on a broken pier and forced his breathing to slow. Counted his heartbeats. The phase lag in his body was worse today—orders arriving later, movements landing at odd intervals. Walking felt like operating a puppet of himself. And his left hand was changing: the copper-gold glow had spread past his wrist, creeping up his forearm, and the skin underneath felt wrong. Hot. Tight. Like something was trying to break through from inside.",
                "type": "file"
              },
              "start_ix": 9425
            },
            {
              "citation_format_type": "berry_file_search",
              "end_ix": 9542,
              "metadata": {
                "extra": {
                  "authors_display": null,
                  "cited_message_id": "b7f3abaa-13b5-450f-903a-0edae8516c93",
                  "cited_message_idx": 67,
                  "cloud_doc_url": null,
                  "doi": null,
                  "journal_homepage_url": null,
                  "journal_name": null,
                  "line_range": [
                    9,
                    12
                  ],
                  "publication_year": "None",
                  "retrieval_file_index": 2,
                  "retrieval_turn": 5,
                  "search_result_idx": 2
                },
                "id": "file_00000000be0471f8be6b30e111ea6ee7",
                "name": "N02_ LIGHT CRACKS THROUGH (1).md",
                "source": "my_files",
                "text": "He looked at his left hand. The copper-gold glow had spread to his elbow. The skin underneath was pale, almost translucent, like something was happening at a level his eyes couldn't quite focus on.\n*Cost mutation visible. Spread rate: \\~handspan per Tide.
